titulo: Ten years on, Belgium drops case against Fortis ex-directors

titulo: Asia's central banks have almost no reasons to hike rates in 2019
resumo: Weaker economic growth and tepid price pressures in Asia will leave central banks in the region with few reasons to tighten policy next year -- especially as U.S. rate hikes slow -- unless a new bout of currency weakness forces them to do so.

titulo: Recharge: LG and Samsung take heart as China flags more open car battery market
resumo: For automotive battery makers LG Chem Ltd  and Samsung SDI Co Ltd , the Chinese market has begun to offer promise after painful loss-making investments.

titulo: Aluminum plunges, Rusal shares soar as U.S. to lift sanctions
resumo: The U.S. Treasury said it will lift sanctions on the core empire of Russian businessman Oleg Deripaska, including aluminum giant Rusal and its parent En+, watering down the toughest penalties imposed since Moscow's 2014 annexation of Crimea.

titulo: Novartis appoints Susanne Schaffert to lead oncology business
resumo: Novartis  said it appointed company veteran Susanne Schaffert to lead its oncology unit from Jan. 1, replacing Liz Barrett who is stepping down to return to the United States.

titulo: China bike-sharing firm Ofo faces 'immense' cash crunch, CEO vows to battle on
resumo: Chinese bike-sharing startup Ofo, backed by Alibaba Group Holding Ltd , is battling "immense" cash flow problems and disbanding the firm has been considered as an option, its chief executive said in a letter to employees.

titulo: In Asia, nascent men's make-up market starts drawing big brands
resumo: From an eye shadow that opens with the flick of a thumb, Zippo-style, to a barely-there foundation in subtle blue-gray packaging: men's make-up is getting a makeover as manufacturers bid to take it more mainstream.

titulo: Fed raises interest rates, signals more hikes ahead
resumo: After weeks of market volatility and calls by President Donald Trump for the Federal Reserve to stop raising interest rates, the U.S. central bank instead did it again, and stuck by a plan to keep withdrawing support from an economy it views as strong.

titulo: Noble Group completes $3.5 billion restructuring to emerge as smaller, unlisted firm
resumo: Noble Group , the once mighty commodity trader, on Thursday completed its drawn out $3.5 billion debt restructuring to emerge as a smaller, unlisted Asia-focused coal-trading business.

titulo: Nissan, Renault bosses met one-on-one in Amsterdam, Nissan says
resumo: Nissan Motor Co  CEO Hiroto Saikawa held a one-on-one meeting with the acting boss of automaker partner Renault  in Amsterdam this week that led to "positive" and "productive" talks, the Japanese automaker said on Thursday.

titulo: Exclusive: Renault, Nissan executives sought other ways to pay Ghosn - documents
resumo: Executives from both carmakers in the Renault-Nissan alliance looked at least twice at legal ways to pay Chairman Carlos Ghosn undisclosed income through the partners' shared finances, documents seen by Reuters show.

titulo: SoftBank Corp shares recover from early fall, after record IPO
resumo: SoftBank Corp shares fell as much as 8 percent early on Thursday before erasing the losses in volatile trading, following the telco's 15 percent tumble on debut the previous day.

titulo: Heard on the grapevine: How Pacific trade pact primes New Zealand wine for Japan buzz
resumo: From sleepy New Zealand hillsides to sleek Tokyo dining scenes, a bottle of Hawke's Bay wine shipped to Japan traces an arc that exporters like Nigel Avery see as an avenue for multi-million dollar growth built on a free trade pact.

titulo: Nissan says CEO had one-on-one meeting with Renault's acting boss
resumo: Nissan Motor Co  on Thursday said CEO Hiroto Saikawa had held a one-on-one meeting with the acting boss of its automaking partner Renault SA  during talks between the alliance in Amsterdam this week.

titulo: Oil rebounds from rout on signs of strong product demand
resumo: Oil prices rose on Wednesday, recovering somewhat from a sharp selloff during the previous session, after U.S. data showed strong demand for refined products.

titulo: China's Sinograin confirms U.S. soybean purchases
resumo: China's Sinograin said it had recently bought a few batches of soybeans from the United States, amid a truce in a trade war between the two nations.

titulo: Facebook shares drop as data privacy fallout spreads
resumo: Facebook Inc shares sank on Wednesday as concerns about its ability to safeguard user data sparked a government lawsuit, criticism in the U.S. Congress and a New York Times report on how it had shared data with other companies.

titulo: After Fed selloff, is a U.S. bear market next?
resumo: U.S. stocks took another body blow on Wednesday after the Federal Reserve raised interest rates again and the central bank chairman did not soften his tone about the outlook for further financial tightening to the degree investors had hoped.

titulo: Elon Musk unveils his first Los Angeles-area tunnel
resumo: (This Dec. 18 story corrects paragraphs 2 and 17 to show that free rides were given at the event, not that there were no free rides)

titulo: U.S. imposes fresh Russia sanctions for election meddling
resumo: The United States imposed fresh Russia-related sanctions on Wednesday, expanding a blacklist of individuals allegedly involved in a Kremlin-backed campaign to meddle with the 2016 U.S. presidential election, among other misdeeds.

titulo: A Fed tally: Nearing normal, central bank won a few, and lost as well
resumo: The U.S. Federal Reserve has begun signaling the end of its rate-hike cycle is not far off, winding down its sometimes Sisyphean effort to restore a semblance of normalcy to monetary policy. It has been a slow slog - the Fed has managed just nine increases in three years, and it may squeeze in just a couple more.

titulo: Wall St. tumbles as investors fret over Fed policy update
resumo: U.S. stocks declined sharply on Wednesday after the Federal Reserve's forecast of fewer interest-rate increases in 2019 fell short of investors' hopes of a more dovish monetary policy.

titulo: Japan's Nidec to expand electric-car motor production outside China: Nikkei
resumo: Nidec Corp  will add locations outside China to build motors for electric vehicles as the Japanese motor maker rethinks its supply strategy, the Nikkei business daily reported on Thursday.

titulo: Pier 1 CEO steps down after turnaround efforts fail
resumo: Home furnishing retailer Pier 1 Imports Inc  said on Wednesday its Chief Executive Officer Alasdair James has stepped down and the company is looking at various options as it struggles with falling sales.

titulo: U.S. Congress will not pass self-driving car bill in 2018: senators
resumo: The U.S. Congress will not vote on a bill to speed the introduction of self-driving cars before it adjourns for the year, a blow to companies like General Motors Co and Alphabet Inc's Waymo unit, key senators said on Wednesday.

titulo: LME to remove suspension on Rusal metal if sanctions lifted
resumo: The London Metal Exchange (LME) said on Wednesday it proposed to lift its suspension on aluminum produced by Russia's Rusal if U.S. sanctions are lifted.

titulo: Global stocks sink after Fed hike, bonds rally
resumo: Global equity markets gave up earlier gains and continued a weeklong sell-off on Wednesday after the U.S. Federal Reserve announced a fresh interest rate hike and said "some" further rate hikes would be necessary in the year ahead.

titulo: Brewer AB InBev partners with Tilray to tap cannabis drink market
resumo: Anheuser-Busch InBev , the world's largest brewer, and Canadian pot producer Tilray Inc  are partnering in a $100-million joint venture to research cannabis-infused non-alcoholic drinks for the Canadian market, the companies said Wednesday.

titulo: Buffett profits as venture in Canadian mortgage business ends
resumo: Billionaire investor Warren Buffett is cutting ties with a  Canadian mortgage business after a nifty trade that provided the company with a potential lifeline.

titulo: Exclusive: Russia's Deripaska prepares to cede control of Rusal, En+ - sources
resumo: Russian tycoon Oleg Deripaska is preparing to cede control of Rusal  and its parent En+ , moving part of his En+ stake to a blind trust, as talks with the United States on lifting sanctions advance, three sources familiar with the talks said.

titulo: Instant View: Stocks sink after Fed raises rates, Powell comments on balance sheet
resumo: U.S. stock markets tumbled on Wednesday, reversing a morning rally after the U.S. Federal Reserve delivered an expected interest rate hike but signaled its tightening cycle is nearing an end in the face of financial market volatility and slowing global growth.

titulo: El Sharara oilfield to reopen, Libyan government says
resumo: Libya's biggest oilfield, El Sharara, will reopen, the country's    internationally recognized government said on Wednesday after   Prime Minister Fayez al-Sarraj flew there to persuade protesters to end a blockage.

titulo: Exclusive: Exxon Mobil secured U.S. hardship waiver from biofuels laws - sources
resumo: The U.S. Environmental Protection Agency granted oil major  Exxon Mobil Corp  a financial hardship waiver this year temporarily freeing its Montana refinery from U.S. biofuel laws, three sources familiar with the matter told Reuters.

titulo: Payments company Square to re-apply for a bank license
resumo: Square Inc , the payments company led by Twitter Inc  chief executive Jack Dorsey, is refiling its application with U.S. state and federal regulators for a bank license, the company said on Wednesday.

titulo: Brazil court ruling casts doubt over Petrobras asset sales
resumo: A Brazilian Supreme Court justice on Wednesday suspended a presidential decree establishing rules for Petroleo Brasileiro SA  to sell upstream assets, casting a pall over the state-controlled oil company's ambitious divestiture program.

titulo: Shell forms joint venture to produce offshore wind energy in New Jersey
resumo: EDF Renewables North America said on Wednesday it had formed a joint venture with oil and gas firm Royal Dutch Shell plc's  new energies division to co-develop a lease area for offshore wind energy in New Jersey.

titulo: Uber loses latest UK legal bid to block worker rights for drivers
resumo: Uber has lost its latest court bid to stop its British drivers being classified as workers, entitling them to rights such as the minimum wage, in a decision which jeopardizes the taxi app's business model.

titulo: U.S. existing home sales rise, trend weak
resumo: U.S. home sales unexpectedly rose in November, but recorded their biggest annual decline in 7-1/2 years as the housing market remained mired in weakness amid higher mortgage rates which have made home purchases more expensive.

titulo: Another technical tweak as Fed wrestles to control policy rate
resumo: The U.S. Federal Reserve continued its quiet battle to keep a firm grip on its policy rate on Wednesday by again narrowing the gap between the effective ceiling and floor that keeps it contained.

titulo: Altria nears Juul stake deal, valuing it at $38 billion: sources
resumo: Marlboro cigarette maker Altria Group Inc  is nearing an agreement to buy more than a third of Juul Labs Inc, valuing the e-cigarette startup at $38 billion, people familiar with the matter said on Wednesday.

titulo: Malaysia charges another ex-Goldman Sachs banker over 1MDB
resumo: Malaysia on Wednesday charged a second former banker at Goldman Sachs Group Inc  in connection with a suspected money-laundering scheme at state fund 1MDB and said it had issued a summons to the bank.

titulo: Data problems complicate U.S. regulator's watch over ratings firms
resumo: A decade since the global financial crisis, the U.S. securities regulator is still struggling to get a grip on the country's rating agencies because it lacks the tools and expertise needed to crunch growing reams of ratings data, people familiar with the effort say.

titulo: SoftBank telco suffers $9 billion slump on debut after record IPO
resumo: SoftBank Corp  shares sank 15 percent on debut, wiping $9 billion off their value, as investors sold off the telecoms operator after its record IPO on worries about a recent service outage and its exposure to Chinese telecoms gear maker Huawei.

titulo: FedEx shares drop as economic slowdown prompts 2019 profit forecast cut
resumo: Shares of FedEx Corp  fell nearly 8 percent on Wednesday, after the delivery package company jolted investors with a steeper-than-expected cut in its 2019 profit forecast warning of weakening freight demand as the global economy slows.

titulo: Facebook says companies got access to data only after user permission
resumo: Facebook Inc  said it did not give companies access to personal data of users without their permission, after the New York Times reported on Tuesday that the social network allowed some tech giants far greater access to data than it had disclosed.

titulo: Facebook's lead EU regulator 'assessing' NYT report on data sharing
resumo: Facebook's  lead regulator in the European Union on Wednesday said it was assessing a report in the New York Times that the social network allowed some tech companies far greater access to data than it had disclosed.

titulo: General Mills second-quarter profit jumps on tighter costs, price hikes
resumo: General Mills Inc  reported a quarterly profit above Wall Street estimates on Wednesday as the maker of Cheerios cereal  raised prices and eked out higher margins from cost savings.

titulo: Daimler told regulators about coolant-related recall: kfz-betrieb
resumo: Daimler  told European regulators late last month that it will have to recall 127,847 Mercedes vehicles to replace the R134a coolant, trade publication kfz-betrieb reported on Wednesday, not citing the source of its information.

titulo: Travel agents seek EU antitrust probe into Lufthansa pricing
resumo: A group whose members include Amadeus  and Expedia , stepped up their fight against German airline Lufthansa  on Wednesday, urging EU regulators to investigate booking fees it levies on travel agents.

titulo: Volkswagen buys Volvo's connected car unit for $122 million
resumo: Volkswagen (VW)  is buying a controlling stake in Volvo's  WirelessCar, a digital service developer specializing in connected driving, for 1.1 billion Swedish crowns ($122 million), the companies said on Wednesday.

titulo: Drugmaker GSK to split after striking Pfizer consumer health deal
resumo: GlaxoSmithKline plans to split into two businesses -- one for prescription drugs and vaccines, the other for over-the-counter products -- after forming a new joint venture with Pfizer's consumer health division.

titulo: Morgan Stanley moves to speed up collaboration with tech startups
resumo: Morgan Stanley is simplifying the way it acquires new technologies from small companies to speed up collaboration and address long running concerns by startups that working with big banks is slow and costly.

titulo: MetLife settles Massachusetts case over unpaid pensions
resumo: MetLife Inc will pay a $1 million fine to resolve claims that it made misleading statements to investors in failing to pay pension benefits to thousands of retirees it improperly treated as "presumed dead."

titulo: Chip gear makers take a beating after Micron's dour forecast
resumo: Shares of chip gear makers fell after a tepid forecast by U.S.-based Micron Technology Inc  exacerbated fears that a two-year chip boom was fizzling due to sluggish demand from makers of smartphones, PCs and servers.

titulo: Estonia arrests ten in Danske Bank money laundering inquiry
resumo: Estonia has arrested 10 former employees of the local branch of Danske Bank  as part of an international investigation into alleged money laundering.

titulo: Huawei to spend $2 billion over five years in cybersecurity push
resumo: (This version of the December 18th story corrects chairman's last name to Hu in 11th, 14th and last paragraphs)

titulo: Thyssenkrupp to propose Merz as chairwoman as year of turmoil ends
resumo: Thyssenkrupp  will propose as chairwoman Martina Merz, whose background includes roles at Volvo , Bosch [ROBG.UL] and Lufthansa , the German company said, as it prepares to spin off its capital goods units.

titulo: German auto supplier to plead guilty, pay $35 million fine in VW emissions case
resumo: German auto supplier IAV Gmbh has agreed to plead guilty and pay a $35 million fine for conspiring to assist Volkswagen AG  in its effort to evade U.S. diesel emissions standards, the U.S. Justice Department said on Tuesday.

titulo: Exxon Mobil opposes weakening Obama-era emissions rules: letter to EPA
resumo: Exxon Mobil Corp  sent a letter to the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency in support of methane gas emission rules put in place under the Obama administration, according to a copy of the letter seen by Reuters.

titulo: Renault-Nissan leaders to meet amid alliance crisis
resumo: The head of Nissan and acting CEO of Renault will hold talks during a two day meeting of their carmaking partnership starting on Tuesday, sources said, amid a crisis in relations sparked by the arrest of long-time alliance supremo Carlos Ghosn.

titulo: BP launches $3 billion sale of U.S. onshore assets to fund BHP deal: sources
resumo: Britain's BP has launched the sale of U.S. oil and gas onshore assets that could raise more than $3 billion to help pay for other fields in the United States it bought in October from BHP , industry and banking sources said.

titulo: J&J says Indian drug regulators visited facilities, company provided talc samples
resumo: Johnson & Johnson said on Wednesday that Indian drugs authorities visited some of its facilities and it provided them with "tests and samples" of its talcum powder.

titulo: Bristol-Myers gets $1.6 billion offer for French consumer health unit
resumo: Bristol-Myers Squibb Co  received an offer from Japanese healthcare firm Taisho Pharmaceutical Holdings Co Ltd  to buy the company's French over-the-counter drugs business UPSA for $1.6 billion, the companies said on Wednesday.

titulo: Boeing says Indian airfares unsustainably low; lifts country's 20-year order forecast
resumo: Airfares in the fast-growing Indian market are 10 to 15 percent lower than breakeven levels for airlines, a Boeing Co  executive said, as the planemaker raised its long-term jet order forecast for the nation to a record despite market challenges.

titulo: Asian business confidence wallows near three-year low on trade worries: Thomson Reuters/INSEAD
resumo: A very cautious optimism remains among Asian companies in the fourth quarter as they wait to see whether there will be any breakthrough in a trade dispute between the United States and China, a Thomson Reuters/INSEAD survey showed.

titulo: Italy's transport minister says he would like to revoke Autostrade concession
resumo: Italy's Transport Minister Danilo Toninelli said on Wednesday that he would like to revoke motorway concessions held by Autostrade per l'Italia  after the collapse of a bridge in Genoa last August.

titulo: Indian regulator to test samples of Johnson & Johnson baby powder: media
resumo: India's federal drug regulator will on Wednesday seize samples of Johnson & Johnson's  baby powder for testing, local media said, following a Reuters report that the company knew for decades that cancer-causing asbestos lurked in the product.

titulo: Investors revolt against executive pay plans at tarnished Australian banks
resumo: Shareholders voted against the executive remuneration plans of two of Australia's biggest banks on Wednesday following misconduct revelations that have swept through the country's financial sector.

titulo: Micron sales, profit miss estimates as chip glut hurts prices
resumo: U.S. chipmaker Micron Technology Inc  gave on Tuesday quarterly sales and profit forecasts well below Wall Street estimates, citing a market glut of memory chips as consumer and business demand for phones and computers is weakening.

titulo: Sears to take $443 million in charges arising from store closures
resumo: Bankrupt U.S. retailer Sears Holdings Corp will take a charge of about $443 million arising from store closures, a regulatory filing showed on Tuesday.

titulo: Oil drops over 5 percent on economic slowdown fears, supply glut
resumo: Oil prices tumbled more than 5 percent on Tuesday in heavy trade due to fears of oversupply and deteriorating demand, extending a selloff that has taken major crude benchmarks down more than 30 percent from an October peak.

titulo: FedEx sees global trade slowdown, says U.S. economy still 'solid'
resumo: FedEx Corp  on Tuesday slashed its 2019 forecast after Europe's economy weakened and the U.S. trade row exacerbated a slowdown in China, sending shares in the package delivery company tumbling more than 6 percent after the closing bell.

titulo: High taxes could hasten bank moves from Britain after Brexit
resumo: Britain risks driving banks overseas if current high levels of taxation on the industry are maintained after Brexit, a bank lobby group said on Wednesday.

titulo: S&P flat as government shutdown threat, Fed decision loom
resumo: The benchmark S&P 500 stock index ended little changed in a choppy trading session on Tuesday as the possibility of a partial U.S. government shutdown raised investor jitters ahead of a highly anticipated meeting of the Federal Reserve.

titulo: J&J moves to limit impact of Reuters report on asbestos in Baby Powder
resumo: Johnson & Johnson on Monday scrambled to contain fallout from a Reuters report that the healthcare conglomerate knew for decades that cancer-causing asbestos lurked in its Baby Powder, taking out full-page newspaper ads defending its product and practices, and readying its chief executive for his first television interview since investors erased tens of billions of dollars from the company’s market value.

titulo: Lockheed Martin wins $585 million U.S. defense contract: Pentagon
resumo: Lockheed Martin Corp  has been awarded a $585 million U.S. defense contract for a Homeland Defense Radar in Hawaii, the Pentagon said on Tuesday.

titulo: Judge mulls using monitor to oversee CVS during court process
resumo: A federal judge said on Tuesday that he was considering using a court-appointed monitor to make sure CVS Health Corp refrains from fully integrating with insurer Aetna while he examines the companies' settlement with the government.

titulo: Yelp, Consumer Watchdog lose Google intervention bid
resumo: Search company Yelp and lobby group Consumer Watchdog lost their joint bid to intervene in Google's challenge of a 2.4 billion euro ($2.7 billion) EU antitrust fine after a court said they had no direct interest in the case.

titulo: Uber gets approval from Pennsylvania to resume self-driving testing
resumo: Uber Technologies Inc received approval from Pennsylvania to resume self-driving car testing on public roads this week, state records show, nine months after it suspended the program following a deadly accident in Arizona.

titulo: Trump pressures Fed before meeting, warns against 'another mistake'
resumo: U.S. President Donald Trump on Tuesday further sought to pressure the Federal Reserve as the central bank prepared to start its two-day policy meeting, warning the Fed's board not to "make yet another mistake" ahead of an expected interest rate hike.

titulo: Stocks dip ahead of Fed news; oil prices drop
resumo: Global equity markets dipped on Tuesday as nervous investors awaited indications whether the Federal Reserve will be able to raise interest rates much further amid turbulent markets and a weakening outlook for the global economy.

titulo: Glencore's Katanga pays $22 million to settle Canada regulator probe
resumo: Glencore-controlled  Katanga Mining Ltd  said on Tuesday it agreed to pay more than $22 million to settle Canadian allegations of inadequate historical disclosures of its finances and activities in the Democratic Republic of Congo.

titulo: China embarks on second round of soy buying post Trump-Xi truce
resumo: Chinese importers returned to the U.S. soy market on Tuesday for their second round of purchases since the two countries agreed to a truce in their trade war, four traders with knowledge of the deals and the U.S. Soybean Export Council said.

titulo: Kinder Morgan faces proposed class action lawsuit on pipeline
resumo: Kinder Morgan is facing a proposed class action lawsuit, claiming that a division of the pipeline operator has emitted chemicals and toxic gas that caused damage to property and killed animals.

titulo: CBS fires CEO Leslie Moonves and denies $120 million severance
resumo: CBS Corp said on Monday it has fired Leslie Moonves for cause and has denied a $120 million severance package as it girds for a potential legal battle with its former chief executive who has been accused of sexual harassment and assault that allegedly took place before and after he joined the company.

titulo: Citi reorganizes prime brokerage unit
resumo: Citigroup Inc  is shuffling its prime brokerage business, according to an internal memo seen by Reuters, amid reports that the unit could lose millions on a bad loan to an Asian hedge fund.

titulo: IPO activity next year could dampen if markets do not rebound: report
resumo: The excitement of proposed initial public offerings by ride-hailing rivals Uber and Lyft next year may not be enough to encourage other firms to follow suit, as they fear that slowing global growth and rocky stock markets dragging into next year could threaten market debut valuations.

titulo: Treasury's Mnuchin says expects January trade talks with China: Bloomberg
resumo: The United States and China are planning to hold meetings in January to "document an agreement" on trade, U.S. Treasury Secretary Steven Mnuchin told Bloomberg in an interview on Tuesday.

titulo: U.S. agency to speed reviews of automaker self-driving petitions
resumo: The U.S. auto safety regulator said on Tuesday it is speeding up the process for reviewing whether automakers can deploy self-driving vehicles without devices such as brakes and steering wheels.

titulo: Musk's SpaceX to raise $500 million in funding: WSJ
resumo: Elon Musk's rocket company SpaceX Corp is set to raise $500 million at a $30.5 billion valuation, the Wall Street Journal reported on Tuesday, citing people familiar with the fundraising plan.

titulo: GM cutting 50 jobs at U.S. battery plant tied to Chevy Volt
resumo: General Motors Co  said on Tuesday it plans to cut 50 jobs at a suburban Detroit lithium-ion battery assembly plant after the largest U.S. automaker announced last month it will end production of its plug-in hybrid Chevrolet Volt in 2019.

titulo: Facebook's Sandberg cites need to do more after reports of Russia meddling
resumo: Facebook Inc's Chief Operating Officer Sheryl Sandberg said on Tuesday the company needs to step up its efforts to curb misinformation after research revealed that Russia-backed organizations targeted African-Americans, who favored Hillary Clinton, to suppress voter turnout during the 2016 election.

titulo: Burnout, stress lead more companies to try a four-day work week
resumo: (Corrects paragraph 4 to identify Perpetual Guardian as a trust company instead of an insurance company; corrects paragraph 13 to reflect that the study was conducted by Kronos and Future Workplace)

titulo: Volkswagen may have to step up electric car plans to meet EU CO2 targets
resumo: Volkswagen  may have to step up its plans for mass production of electric vehicles in order to meet tougher-than-expected European targets to cut greenhouse emissions from cars, its chief executive said on Tuesday.

titulo: Twitter tumbles on concerns about hacking activity
resumo: Twitter Inc  shares fell almost 7 percent on Monday after the company said it was investigating unusual traffic that might be from state-sponsored hackers and, in what appeared to be an unrelated issue, a security firm said hackers used the platform to try to steal user data.

titulo: Kroger starts use of unmanned vehicles for delivery in Arizona
resumo: U.S. supermarket chain Kroger Co  said on Tuesday it has started using unmanned autonomous vehicles to deliver groceries Scottsdale, Arizona in partnership with Silicon Valley startup Nuro.

titulo: Indian regulator says Reuters report on J&J baby powder 'under consideration'
resumo: India's federal drug regulator said on Tuesday a Reuters report that Johnson & Johnson  knew for decades that cancer-causing asbestos lurked in its baby powder was "under consideration".

titulo: Malaysia says Goldman Sachs failed to disclose key facts in 1MDB bond sales
resumo: Goldman Sachs Group Inc  made untrue statements and omitted key facts in offering circulars for the bonds it sold for Malaysian state fund 1MDB, the Malaysian government said in criminal charges against the U.S. bank.

titulo: Cash-strapped South African Airways expands Emirates code sharing
resumo: South African Airways (SAA) said on Tuesday that it had signed a deal with Emirates [EMIRA.UL] to expand an existing codeshare agreement, in a rare bright spot for the cash-strapped airline.

titulo: Italy's UniCredit settles dispute with Caius
resumo: British hedge fund Caius has made a payment to UniCredit  to settle a dispute over complex financial instruments which Italy's biggest bank counts toward its core capital, the two companies said.

titulo: T-Mobile, Sprint win U.S. security approvals for merger
resumo: T-Mobile US Inc and Sprint Corp have won backing for their $26 billion merger from two national security reviews on Monday, clearing key hurdles in their tie-up bid.

titulo: South Korea to provide $3 billion in financial support for troubled auto suppliers
resumo: South Korea unveiled a policy package to support its embattled car parts makers industry that includes providing financial support worth over 3.5 trillion won ($3.10 billion) and encouraging the use of electric cars.

titulo: T-Mobile, Sprint get CFIUS approval for proposed merger
resumo: T-Mobile US  and Sprint Corp  have received approval from the Committee on Foreign Investment in the United States (CFIUS) for their proposed merger, the companies said on Monday.

titulo: Qualcomm says Apple violating Chinese court order, despite new software
resumo: Qualcomm Inc said it believes Apple Inc remains in violation of a Chinese court's orders to stop selling iPhones despite a software update that Apple pushed on Monday.

titulo: S&P 500 hits 14-month low on economic jitters ahead of Fed meeting
resumo: Wall Street's major indexes all slid more than 2 percent on Monday, with the benchmark S&P 500 closing at its lowest in 14 months, on concerns about slowing economic growth ahead of a highly anticipated decision from the Federal Reserve this week on the course of U.S. interest-rate hikes.

titulo: Oracle sees strong third quarter on cloud strength, share rise
resumo: Oracle Corp on Monday forecast current-quarter profit above estimates after growth in its cloud services and license support unit helped the business software maker surpass Wall Street expectations for the second quarter.

titulo: Google to spend $1 billion on new campus in New York
resumo: Alphabet Inc's  Google is investing more than $1 billion on a new campus in New York, becoming the second major technology company after Amazon to pick America's financial capital to expand and create thousands of jobs.

titulo: Embraer's net proceeds from Boeing deal the same, despite higher price
resumo: Brazil's Embraer SA will receive the same net proceeds from a deal to sell 80 percent of its commercial jet unit to Boeing Co, even though the U.S. aircraft maker increased the enterprise value which includes debt by 11 percent.

titulo: DoubleLine's Gundlach says U.S. equities are in long-term bear market
resumo: Jeffrey Gundlach, chief executive of DoubleLine Capital, on Monday said the S&P 500 stock index is headed to new lows and that U.S. equities are in a long-term bear market.

titulo: J&J announces $5 billion share buyback after shares extend losses
resumo: Johnson & Johnson said on Monday it plans to buy back up to $5 billion of its stock, after a Reuters report on Friday that the company knew for decades that its Baby Powder contained cancer-causing asbestos wiped about $40 billion from its market value.

titulo: Oil falls, U.S. crude dips below $50 on oversupply fears
resumo: Oil prices fell more than 2 percent on Monday, with U.S. crude tumbling below $50 a barrel, on signs of oversupply in the United States and as investor concern over global economic growth and fuel demand grows.

titulo: J&J announces $5 billion share repurchase plan
resumo: Johnson & Johnson  on Monday announced a share repurchase of up to $5 billion of its common stock.  The repurchase plan has no time limit and may be suspended for periods or discontinued at any time, the company said in a statement.

titulo: General Dynamics warns Canada: Canceling Saudi deal would cost billions
resumo: General Dynamics Corp  on Monday put pressure on Ottawa over the sale of armored vehicles to Saudi Arabia, warning that the federal government would incur "billions of dollars of liability" by unilaterally scrapping the deal.

titulo: Global stocks drop, where is 'Santa Claus rally'?
resumo: Broad stock declines in Europe and the United States dragged world equity markets lower on Monday, adding to a sell-off that has sent global stocks near 17-month lows.

titulo: Goldman Sachs fires back after Malaysia charges bank in 1MDB probe
resumo: Malaysia on Monday filed criminal charges against Goldman Sachs Group Inc related to its dealings with the sovereign wealth fund 1MDB, and Goldman Sachs fired back that the previous Malaysian government had lied to the investment bank.

titulo: U.S. healthcare stocks drop after judge rules Obamacare unconstitutional
resumo: Shares of U.S. health insurers, hospitals and healthcare companies fell on Monday in the aftermath of a ruling by a federal judge in Texas that the Affordable Care Act (ACA), commonly called Obamacare, was unconstitutional.

titulo: Carlyle nearing deal to buy StandardAero for more than $5 billion: sources
resumo: Private equity firm Carlyle Group LP  is nearing a deal to acquire aviation services company StandardAero from Veritas Capital, another buyout firm, for more than $5 billion, including debt, people familiar with the matter said on Monday.

titulo: Siemens, Alstom offer to sell high-speed train technology: sources
resumo: Siemens  and Alstom  have offered to sell either one of their high-speed train technology to address EU antitrust concerns about their plan to create a Franco-German rail champion, people familiar with the matter said on Monday.

titulo: U.S. ethanol producers seek pricing reform as markets plunge, ADM sells
resumo: U.S. ethanol producers stung by collapsing prices are seeking changes to the way benchmark values for the biofuel are established, arguing the current system used by exchanges is vulnerable to manipulation, according to sources.

titulo: BNY Mellon to pay more than $54 million for improper handling of ADRs: SEC
resumo: The top U.S. securities regulator on Monday said that Bank of New York Mellon  will pay more than $54 million to settle charges of improper handling of "pre-released" American Depositary Receipts, U.S. securities representing foreign shares of a foreign company.

titulo: Czech cyber watchdog calls Huawei, ZTE products a security threat
resumo: The Czech cyber watchdog warned network operators on Monday against using software or hardware made by Chinese telecom equipment suppliers Huawei and ZTE , saying they posed a security threat.

titulo: Goldman, Morgan Stanley gain clout in Brazil as NY IPOs grow
resumo: Goldman Sachs Group Inc , Morgan Stanley and Bank of America Merrill Lynch are regaining clout in Latin America's largest economy as Brazilian companies opt to list shares in New York instead of on their local exchanges, Refinitiv data shows.

titulo: Nissan CEO calls on Renault to listen to detail of Ghosn allegations
resumo: Nissan Motor's CEO called on directors at alliance partner Renault to heed its reasons for sacking former Chairman Carlos Ghosn over alleged financial misconduct amid tensions over how to handle the fallout from his arrest.

titulo: China, EU lambast United States for miring WTO in crisis
resumo: The United States came under fire from China and the European Union on Monday, accused by both major trading partners of taking protectionist measures and bringing the World Trade Organization (WTO) to its knees.

titulo: China says U.S. has brought back 'the ghost of unilateralism' to WTO
resumo: China said on Monday that the Trump administration's trade measures on items from steel to intellectual property taken under the guise national security were "bringing back to life the ghost of unilateralism".

titulo: Eni, Shell knew of 'sharks' in Nigeria graft case: judge
resumo: An Italian judge said on Monday oil majors Eni  and Royal Dutch Shell  were fully aware their 2011 purchase of a Nigerian oilfield would result in corrupt payments to Nigerian politicians and officials.

titulo: German union calls strike at Amazon warehouses
resumo: German trade union Verdi called on workers at two Amazon  logistic centers to strike on Monday, part of a long-running campaign for better pay and conditions.

titulo: ABB to sell Power Grids to Hitachi, revamp business structure
resumo: ABB  reached a deal with Japan's Hitachi  to sell its Power Grids division, with the Swiss engineering group adding it plans to return net cash proceeds of $7.6-7.8 billion from selling the 80.1 percent stake in the unit to shareholders.

titulo: Foxconn not in settlement talks with Qualcomm in Apple battle: attorney
resumo: The lead attorney for the group of Apple Inc device assemblers seeking at least $9 billion in damages from Qualcomm Inc said on Sunday the contract manufacturers are not in settlement talks with the mobile chip supplier and are "gearing up and heading toward the trial" in April.

titulo: As bitcoin trading shifts shape, big money stays away
resumo: (This version of the December 7th story corrects spelling of first name to Erik (not Eric) in paragraph 21)

titulo: Ten years on, Fed's long, strange, trip to zero redefined central banking
resumo: Whatever the acronym, when the U.S. Federal Reserve dropped its policy rate to near zero on Dec. 16, 2008, to counter a full-scale economic crisis, it ushered in what the central bank's chairman at the time, Ben Bernanke, called "the end of the old regime."

titulo: Mercedes-Benz cuts China prices after tariff on U.S.-made cars suspended
resumo: German luxury carmaker Mercedes-Benz on Monday said it would offer discounts on cars imported into China from the United States until March 31 after Beijing suspended additional tariffs on U.S.-made cars.

titulo: Nissan hopes Renault will listen about Ghosn misconduct
resumo: Nissan's  Chief Executive said on Monday he hoped alliance partner Renault SA  will listen to its explanation of financial misconduct by former Chairman Carlos Ghosn.

titulo: German regional state to sue VW over diesel scandal: FAZ
resumo: The German state of Baden-Wuerttemberg plans to sue Volkswagen over the carmaker's cheating of emissions tests, newspaper Frankfurter Allgemeine Zeitung (FAZ) reported on Monday.

titulo: Nestle to cut about 380 jobs at four locations in Germany
resumo: Swiss food giant Nestle  is cutting around 380 jobs in Germany by closing two locations and reducing the workforce at two others as it reacts to lower demand and shifts some production elsewhere, the company said on Monday.

titulo: Canadian regulator to fine Glencore-controlled miner over Congo - WSJ
resumo: A Glencore-controlled  mining company and some of its current and former executives have agreed to pay more than $22 million to settle Canadian allegations they hid the risks of doing business with an Israeli close to Congolese President Joseph Kabila, the Wall Street Journal reported.

titulo: Uber welcomes, unions criticize UK plan to maintain flexible gig economy
resumo: Uber Technologies Inc welcomed on Monday the British government's response to a review into workplace rights, which looks set to preserve the business models of gig economy companies, but unions rubbished the business ministry's plans.

titulo: ABB relents to activist shareholder and unloads Power Grids unit
resumo: ABB  will sell a majority stake in its Power Grids division to Japan's Hitachi  and return the proceeds to shareholders, bowing to pressure from an activist investor to sell the business that makes transformers and converters.

titulo: Canada gets consular access to second man detained in China
resumo: Canadian diplomats gained consular access on Sunday to the second of two men detained by China over the past week, Canada's foreign ministry said in a statement that gave few details, as China said it was ensuring their rights were protected.

titulo: Exclusive: Nissan probe into Ghosn trains lens on 'CEO Reserve' fund, Dutch unit
resumo: (This December 15 story was refiled to correct paragraph 9 to show date Nissan fired Ghosn as chairman as Nov. 22, not Nov. 26)

titulo: Australia bank NAB's CEO to take long holiday around inquiry report release
resumo: National Australia Bank Ltd  said on Monday its CEO will take about two months leave around the scheduled release of a major report into the country's finance sector, prompting a rebuke from a shareholder group.

titulo: Taisho said to near $1.6 billion deal for Bristol-Myers's UPSA: Bloomberg
resumo: Japanese healthcare firm Taisho Pharmaceutical Holdings Co Ltd  is said to be close to a $1.6 billion deal for Bristol-Myers Squibb Co's  French over-the-counter drugs business UPSA, Bloomberg reported on Sunday, citing sources familiar with the matter.

titulo: EQT's board to seek talks with Rice brothers: sources
resumo: EQT Corp's board of directors will ask to meet Toby and Derek Rice for talks, people familiar with the matter said on Sunday, after the two brothers threatened the largest producer of natural gas in the United States with a board challenge.

titulo: Pemex aims for splash in shallow waters, retreats from the deeps
resumo: Mexican state-run oil giant Petroleos Mexicanos will focus on existing shallow water assets and refining next year at the expense of riskier, deepwater projects under a new government that has vowed to turn around the ailing company.

titulo: Renault seeks Nissan shareholder meeting as Ghosn crisis deepens
resumo: Renault  requested a full Nissan shareholder meeting, a source close to the company said - appearing to escalate the carmaker's standoff with its Japanese alliance partner in the wake of the pay scandal engulfing Chairman and CEO Carlos Ghosn.

titulo: Shareholders call on ExxonMobil to set greenhouse gas reduction targets
resumo: A number of institutional investors in ExxonMobil Corp  have said they will file a shareholder resolution which calls on the world's largest oil company to set targets for lowering its greenhouse gas emissions.

titulo: Recent market 'jolt' will be first of many as easy money era ends, says BIS
resumo: Recent sharp selloffs across global financial markets are probably the first of many, as investors adjust to a world of tighter monetary conditions and the threat of economic downturn, the Bank of International Settlements said on Sunday.

titulo: Regulators need joined up thinking to spot derivatives risks: BIS
resumo: Regulators should scrutinize clearing houses and banks together to spot financial stability threats from the world's multi-trillion dollar derivatives market, the Bank for International Settlements said on Sunday.

titulo: Nord Stream 2 can be justified if Ukraine's interests safeguarded
resumo: Germany's economy minister said he believed the Russian-backed Nord Stream 2 pipeline can go ahead while preserving Ukraine's vital interests, but that Berlin is also in talks to diversify its energy supply with liquefied natural gas.

titulo: French government may back Michelin CEO for Renault chairman: paper
resumo: The French government is considering backing Michelin  chief executive Jean-Dominique Senard for the chairmanship of Renault , replacing embattled CEO Carlos Ghosn, Le Figaro reported on Sunday, citing several sources.

titulo: Will landing be soft or 'chaotic' as Fed begins to stop rate hike cycle
resumo: In June 2006, the U.S. Federal Reserve raised interest rates for a 17th consecutive time but cushioned the increase with a strong signal that officials were ready to stop the tightening cycle.

titulo: Renault calls for Nissan shareholder meeting: WSJ
resumo: Renault SA  has sent a letter to its alliance partner Nissan Motor Co Ltd , urging the Japanese carmaker to call a meeting of its shareholders "as promptly as practicable", the Wall Street Journal reported on Sunday.

titulo: Qatar considering increasing its stake in Deutsche Bank: Handelsblatt
resumo: Qatar is considering increasing its stake in Deutsche Bank , newspaper Handelsblatt reported on Sunday.

titulo: Wall Street looks to Fed outlook Wednesday for early Christmas gift
resumo: Investors are eager for a touch of Christmas cheer from the U.S. Federal Reserve next week, hoping for signs the central bank may ease up on interest rate hikes next year and spark a Santa Claus rally.

titulo: Qatar Petroleum to invest $20 billion in U.S. in major expansion
resumo: Qatar Petroleum (QP) is looking to invest at least $20 billion in the United States over the coming few years, its chief executive told Reuters, after the Gulf Arab state unexpectedly quit OPEC this month.

titulo: Indonesia, EFTA sign long delayed free trade deal
resumo: Indonesia on Sunday signed an economic agreement with the European Free Trade Association (EFTA) aimed at increasing trade and investment, concluding almost eight years of negotiations.

titulo: Germany plans to lower threshold for probes of foreign takeovers: Handelsblatt
resumo: Berlin plans to cut to 10 percent the threshold at which it can  launch national security probes of stake purchases by non-European companies in German firms, business newspaper Handelsblatt reported on Sunday.

titulo: 2018: The year volatility came home to roost
resumo: U.S. stocks are down only slightly for 2018, but that masks a volatile year for investors.

titulo: Boeing opens first 737 plant in China amid U.S.-Sino trade war
resumo: Boeing Co  opened its first 737 completion plant in China on Saturday, a strategic investment aimed at building a sales lead over arch-rival Airbus  in one of the world's top travel markets that has been overshadowed by the U.S-China trade war.

titulo: Motiva preliminarily picked to run Curacao refinery: report
resumo: Motiva Enterprises has preliminarily been chosen by the government of Curacao to operate the 335,000-barrel-per-day Isla refinery, replacing Venezuela's state-run PDVSA, local media said on Saturday.

titulo: OPEC has shown it can reach deal despite splits: Iran oil minister
resumo: Iranian Oil Minister Bijan Zanganeh praised OPEC on Saturday for what he said was the producer group's ability to reach agreement despite intense internal political differences.

titulo: Exclusive: T-Mobile, Sprint see Huawei shun clinching U.S. deal - sources
resumo: (This story corrects paragraph 8 to show Wanzhou was arrested in Canada, not China)

titulo: China should free Canadians held after Huawei arrest, U.S.'s Pompeo says
resumo: U.S. Secretary of State Mike Pompeo said on Friday China should free two Canadian citizens who were detained this week after authorities in Canada arrested a senior Chinese technology executive on a U.S. extradition warrant.

titulo: China to halt added tariffs on U.S.-made cars in easing of trade tensions
resumo: China will temporarily suspend additional 25 percent tariffs on U.S.-made vehicles and auto parts starting Jan. 1, 2019, the  finance ministry said on Friday, following a truce in a trade war between the world's two largest economies.

titulo: Tesla cuts China prices after tariff drop on U.S.-made cars
resumo: Tesla Inc  has cut prices on its Model S and Model X vehicles in China after Beijing suspended additional tariffs on U.S.-made cars.

titulo: California regulator opens case against PG&E for falsifying pipeline safety records
resumo: The California Public Utilities Commission has opened a proceeding to consider penalties against Pacific Gas and Electric Company, ordering immediate action against the utility for falsifying safety documents for natural gas pipelines, the regulator said in a statement on Friday.

titulo: Global growth worry hits stocks, but U.S. data lifts dollar
resumo: A gauge of global stocks tumbled on Friday after weak economic data from China and Europe intensified global growth worries as investors weighed the broader impact of the trade dispute between the United States and China.

titulo: CVS defends settlement that allowed Aetna merger as judge assesses
resumo: CVS Health Corp  defended on Friday an agreement with the U.S. Justice Department which allowed it to purchase health insurer Aetna for $69 billion, a settlement that a federal judge is still assessing.

titulo: U.S. sets new March 2 date for China tariff increases amid talks
resumo: The U.S. Trade Representative's office on Friday officially changed the scheduled date of a tariff rate increase on $200 billion worth of Chinese goods to 12:01 a.m. EST (0501 GMT) on March 2, 2019 as the United States and China pursue talks on trade and intellectual property.

titulo: Anti-harassment groups share $20 million CBS handout after Moonves exit
resumo: CBS Corp  on Friday named the Time's Up anti-sexual harassment group and 17 other organizations that will share a $20 million donation stemming from the exit of its chief executive Les Moonves following allegations of sexual misconduct.

titulo: Wall St. tumbles on global growth worries, J&J decline
resumo: Wall Street's three major indexes tumbled on Friday and the Dow confirmed a correction as weak data from China and Europe stoked fears of a global economic slowdown, while Johnson & Johnson shares were the biggest drag after Reuters reported the company knew for decades that its Baby Powder contained asbestos.

titulo: Intel, TPG in early talks to sell McAfee to Thoma Bravo: source
resumo: Private equity firm Thoma Bravo LLC is in early talks to buy security software company McAfee from TPG Capital and Intel Corp, a source familiar with the matter said on Friday.

titulo: Small-cap S&P 600 index confirms bear market
resumo: As U.S. stocks have been rocked by trade tensions and monetary policy worries, shares of small-cap companies, by one measure, have now confirmed that they are in their first bear market in three years.

titulo: J&J shares nosedive on report it knew of asbestos in Baby Powder
resumo: Shares of Johnson & Johnson  fell 10 percent on Friday and were on track to post their biggest percentage drop in more than 16 years, after Reuters reported that the pharma major knew for decades that cancer-causing asbestos lurked in its Baby Powder.

titulo: Irish regulator investigates Facebook over private photo glitch
resumo: An Irish regulator said on Friday it was investigating Facebook Inc  following the company's disclosure that a bug may have exposed private photos of up to 6.8 million users, the latest in a series of Facebook privacy glitches.

titulo: Air Berlin's administrator sues Etihad for up to 2 billion euros
resumo: German airline Air Berlin's insolvency administrator is suing Abu Dhabi's Etihad, its former owner, for damages of up 2 billion euros ($2.26 billion), a Berlin court said on Friday.

titulo: PG&E seeks 6.4 percent rate hike in 2020
resumo: (This Dec. 13 story corrects the rate-hike percentage to 6.4 from 12 in the headline and the first paragraph)

titulo: Canada says it agrees with U.S. on need to keep politics out of Huawei case
resumo: Canada and the United States agreed on Friday that politics should be kept out of the process to extradite senior Huawei Technologies Co [HWT.UL] executive Meng Wanzhou, who is being held on a U.S. extradition warrant, Canadian Foreign Minister Chrystia Freeland said.

titulo: Colombia court fines Odebrecht consortium $250 million for graft
resumo: A Colombian tribunal fined a consortium led by Brazilian construction firm Odebrecht [ODBES.UL] $250 million and banned it from government contracts for 10 years, saying the group used corrupt tactics to win infrastructure contracts.

titulo: Starbucks sales growth to be steady despite UberEats deal, plans for China expansion
resumo: Starbucks Corp  said on Thursday it was partnering with UberEats for delivery from about 3,500 U.S. stores and would nearly double its outlets in China over the next four years, but forecast that same-store sales would remain steady, sending shares down 3 percent.

titulo: LVMH splashes out on luxury hotels with $3.2 billion Belmond deal
resumo: Luxury goods maker LVMH  has agreed to buy Belmond , the owner of hotels including Venice's landmark Cipriani, for a total of $3.2 billion to raise its profile in upmarket hospitality.

titulo: Volkswagen says talks on cooperation with Ford progressing
resumo: Volkswagen's  talks with Ford on the two companies cooperating are progressing, though a few details still need to be worked out, the German carmaker said on Friday.

titulo: Facebook's lead EU regulator opens probe into data breach
resumo: Facebook's  lead regulator in the European Union, the Irish Data Protection Commissioner (DPC), on Friday began an investigation into a number of breach notifications received from the social networking site.

titulo: U.S. consumer spending strengthening in boost to economy
resumo: U.S. consumer spending gathered momentum in November as households bought furniture, electronics and a range of other goods, which could further allay fears of a significant slowdown in the American economy even as the outlook overseas continued to darken.

titulo: Ousted Nissan boss' daughter takes documents, cash from apartment
resumo: The daughter of ousted Nissan chairman Carlos Ghosn early on Friday retrieved documents and cash from a corporate apartment in Rio de Janeiro, capping a legal battle over the items, which the carmaker maintains may contain evidence of wrongdoing.

titulo: N.Y. Fed's U.S. fourth-quarter GDP growth holds near 2.4 percent
resumo: The U.S. economy is growing at a 2.42 percent pace in the fourth quarter as stronger-than-expected November data on industrial output offset the steepest drop in import prices in over three years last month, the New York Federal Reserve's Nowcast model showed on Friday.

titulo: Atlanta Fed raises U.S. fourth-quarter GDP growth view to 3 percent
resumo: The U.S. economy is expanding at a 3.0 percent annualized rate in the fourth quarter based on steady retail sales growth and stronger-than-forecast industrial output in November, the Atlanta Federal Reserve's GDPNow forecast model showed on Friday.

titulo: Trump praises China move on autos, says trade deal could happen soon
resumo: U.S. President Donald Trump welcomed China's move on Friday to suspend additional tariffs on U.S.-made vehicles, saying it could help push forward a larger trade deal with Beijing.

titulo: Macron calls Ford's decision over Bordeaux plant 'hostile'
resumo: French President Emmanuel Macron said on Friday that Ford's  decision to end production at its plant in Blanquefort, southern France next year was "hostile" and said his government was actively seeking a buyer for the site.

titulo: Deutsche Telekom reviews Huawei ties; Orange says no on 5G
resumo: Huawei [HWT.UL] faces fresh challenges in Europe after Germany's Deutsche Telekom  announced it would review its vendor strategy and Orange  said it would not hire the Chinese firm to build its next-generation network in France.

titulo: Scale of theft at Shell's Singapore refinery much greater, court documents show
resumo: Around $150 millions' worth of oil was stolen from Shell's biggest global refinery over several years, Singapore court documents reviewed by Reuters show, far more than reported when police first revealed the heist earlier this year.

titulo: GM to begin U.S. layoff notices as it offers job transfers
resumo: General Motors Co  on Friday said it is beginning to send formal notices to U.S. government agencies of its plan to end production at auto plants and cut thousands of jobs as it shrinks passenger car production in North America.

titulo: LSE names Experian's Donald Robert as next chairman
resumo: London Stock Exchange Group Plc  said on Friday Chairman Donald Brydon would be replaced by Donald Robert, chairman of the world's biggest credit data firm Experian Plc .

titulo: U.S. business inventories increase strongly in October
resumo: - U.S. business inventories rose solidly in October as stocks at retailers increased by the most in more than a year, suggesting inventory investment could contribute to economic growth in the fourth quarter.

titulo: U.S. industrial output up 0.6 percent in November, manufacturing flat
resumo: U.S. industrial output rose in November as a jump in mining and utilities production was offset by drops in other sectors including business equipment and construction.

titulo: Apple to push software update in China as Qualcomm case threatens sales ban
resumo: Apple Inc, facing a court ban in China on some of its iPhone models over alleged infringement of Qualcomm Inc patents, said on Friday it will push software updates to users in a bid to resolve potential issues.

titulo: Exclusive: France plans Renault CEO hunt as board frays over Ghosn - sources
resumo: The French government is seeking candidates to replace Renault's  embattled boss Carlos Ghosn, as some board members began to voice doubts about keeping him in office after his indictment in Japan for suspected misconduct, several sources told Reuters.

titulo: Merck bolsters animal health unit with $2.4 billion Antelliq purchase
resumo: Merck and Co  said on Friday it will buy privately held French company Antelliq Group, which makes digital identification products for livestock, for about 2.1 billion euros ($2.37 billion) to bolster its fast-growing animal health business.

titulo: Renault board did not consider replacing Ghosn: interim chairman
resumo: Renault's board did not consider replacing Chief Executive Carlos Ghosn when it met on Thursday, interim chairman Philippe Lagayette said in a statement issued by the carmaker.

titulo: Exclusive: China Development Bank supervising HNA asset sales - sources
resumo: China Development Bank (CDB) is leading a team to supervise HNA Group's asset disposals as the heavily indebted conglomerate scales back operations to a point that will leave it holding only core assets, people familiar with the situation said.

titulo: SoftBank telco IPO sees strong demand despite Huawei, network disruption
resumo: Domestic investors have sought nearly double the number of shares offered by SoftBank Group Corp's  soon-to-list wireless unit, two sources from a lead IPO underwriter said, underscoring strong demand for Japan's third-largest telco.

titulo: Thyssenkrupp fills key management posts after year of turmoil
resumo: Thyssenkrupp  moved on Friday to fill key management positions, preparing for the spin off its capital goods business after a tumultuous year that saw the resignation of the conglomerate's top leadership.

titulo: Exclusive: Chinese battery firm halts purchases of cobalt from Glencore - sources
resumo: Chinese battery firm GEM has stopped buying cobalt from Glencore as the price of the battery material crashed below that agreed in a three-year deal between the two companies, sources close to the matter said.

titulo: Jet Airways' survival may rest on founder Goyal leaving the cockpit
resumo: Jet Airways Ltd's  69-year-old founder Naresh Goyal, who started out as an assistant in a travel agency, wove together charm, persistence and consummate dealmaking to build India's biggest full-service carrier.

titulo: Exclusive: India's Motherson Sumi in early-stage merger talks with Leoni - sources
resumo: Indian car wiring maker Motherson Sumi Systems Ltd  is in early talks with German peer Leoni AG  over a possible merger of the pair, two people close to the matter said.

titulo: Barclays promotes 85 in investment bank to managing director
resumo: Barclays has promoted 85 executives in the international division that houses its investment bank to the rank of managing director, the British lender said on Friday.

titulo: Thyssenkrupp appoints Walker as new boss of elevator unit
resumo: Industrials conglomerate Thyssenkrupp  on Friday appointed Peter Walker as the new chief executive of its prized elevators unit, the group said.

titulo: Airbus names Mhun as next programs chief
resumo: Airbus  on Friday named services boss Philippe Mhun as its next head of programs, succeeding defense and commercial aerospace veteran Didier Evrard, who is scheduled to retire.

titulo: U.S. recession risks jump, Fed rate hike expectations slump - Reuters poll
resumo: The risk of a U.S. recession in the next two years has risen to 40 percent, according to a Reuters poll of economists who also found a significant shift in expectations toward fewer Federal Reserve interest rate rises next year.

titulo: Nissan files claim against Ghosn's sister in Brazil court
resumo: Nissan Motor Co  has filed a court claim against Carlos Ghosn's sister that references alleged "unjust enrichment", escalating a battle being waged in Brazilian courts between the car maker and its once-celebrated former chairman.

titulo: Nissan board unlikely to select new chairman on Monday: source
resumo: Nissan Motor Co's board is unlikely to select a chairman to succeed Carlos Ghosn as had been expected on Dec. 17 after a panel tasked to choose a candidate did not agree on a nominee in a meeting held Thursday, a source said.

titulo: Explainer: Ex-Nissan chief Ghosn affair raises issues beyond one man, one company
resumo: How big should executive pay packets be? How widespread is lax corporate governance in Japan? And what's the future of the alliance between Nissan and France's Renault?

titulo: Ahead of China anniversary, trade war fans calls for faster market reforms
resumo: China's trade war with the United States is spurring some Chinese entrepreneurs, government advisers and think-tanks to call for faster reforms in the world's second-largest economy and the freeing of a private sector stifled by state controls.

titulo: Japan's business mood, capex steady but outlook sours: BOJ tankan
resumo: Japanese business confidence and capital expenditure plans held steady from three months ago, a closely-watched central bank survey showed, a sign companies weren't significantly worried about escalating trade frictions and global growth concerns.

titulo: Oil prices edge up on hopes global supplies will tighten
resumo: Oil prices climbed more than 2 percent on Thursday, after data showed inventory declines in the United States and as investors began to expect that the global oil market could have a deficit sooner than they had previously thought.

titulo: New York sues Target, Walmart over lead-contaminated toys
resumo: Target Corp , Walmart Inc  and toy importer LaRose Industries were sued on Thursday over the sale of lead-contaminated children's toys, New York Attorney General Barbara Underwood said.

titulo: GE's digital unit to sell majority stake in ServiceMax
resumo: General Electric Co  on Thursday said its digital unit would sell a majority stake in ServiceMax, a cloud-based provider of software used in inventory and workforce management, to technology-focused private equity firm Silver Lake.

titulo: Costco margin dip overshadows sales beat, shares slide
resumo: Costco Wholesale Corp's  gross margins fell in the first quarter, as the warehouse operator paid higher wages, ramped up investments in its online business and cut prices amid intense competition in the grocery industry.

titulo: Evidence of Apple switch ruled inadmissible in Qualcomm antitrust case
resumo: A federal judge on Thursday ruled that evidence provided by chip supplier Qualcomm Inc  that major phone makers like Apple Inc  have moved to competing suppliers like Intel Corp  cannot be used to fight allegations Qualcomm acted to preserve a monopoly on some mobile phone chips.

titulo: Instacart, Amazon's Whole Foods relationship to end next year
resumo: Instacart said on Thursday it will stop delivering groceries for Whole Foods next year, ending a partnership that has been under stress since Amazon.com Inc  bought the upmarket grocer last year.

titulo: Wall Street ends flat as nervous investors flock to defensive shares
resumo: The S&P 500 finished little changed after a volatile session on Thursday, as investors continued to be spooked by uncertainty on both domestic and international issues, driving up bids for defensive shares while financials and consumer discretionary stocks were the biggest drags.

titulo: Long-time GE bear ditches negative view, shares jump
resumo: A JP Morgan analyst who has long held a negative outlook on General Electric Co  shares as they have tumbled upgraded his view on the conglomerate's stock on Thursday.

titulo: Apple plans new $1 billion campus for Austin, Texas
resumo: Apple Inc  said on Thursday it would spend $1 billion to build a second campus in Austin, Texas, that will house up to 15,000 workers as part of a broader push by U.S. companies to create more domestic jobs.

titulo: Renault finds no irregularities in Ghosn pay audit
resumo: Renault  said on Thursday an audit launched in the wake of Chairman and CEO Carlos Ghosn's arrest in Japan had so far found no irregularities with his pay at the French carmaker.

titulo: Japan Post to invest $2.6 billion in U.S. insurer Aflac: source
resumo: Japan Post Holdings Co  is planning to invest about $2.6 billion in Aflac Inc , aiming to become the largest shareholder in the U.S. insurer, a person familiar with the matter said.

titulo: Trump says GM shift to electric vehicles is 'not going to work'
resumo: President Donald Trump on Thursday said General Motors Co's  decision to shift much of its focus to electric vehicles will not succeed, and he asserted a new trade deal will make it harder for the company to move work to other countries.

titulo: Caterpillar CEO Jim Umpleby adds chairman role
resumo: Caterpillar Inc  said on Thursday Chief Executive Officer Jim Umpleby would take on the additional role of chairman, effective immediately.

titulo: Global stocks lose steam as nerves persist, euro dips after ECB
resumo: A gauge of world equities was little changed after giving up early gains on Thursday, continuing a pattern seen for the past several sessions, while the euro eased after the European Central Bank formally ended its bond purchasing scheme.

titulo: Adobe's quarterly revenue surges 23 percent
resumo: Adobe Systems Inc on Thursday reported a 22.8 percent rise in quarterly revenue, helped by higher subscriptions for its flagship Creative Cloud suite of software that includes Photoshop.

titulo: Brazil judge allows ousted Nissan chairman access to Rio apartment
resumo: A Brazilian appellate judge on Thursday granted ousted Nissan Chairman Carlos Ghosn access to a Rio de Janeiro apartment, whose contents have become subject of an intense legal battle in local courts.

titulo: Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan and others back risk management startup AccessFintech
resumo: Goldman Sachs Group Inc , JP Morgan Chase & Co , Citigroup Inc  and Credit Suisse Group AG  have invested $17.5 million in AccessFintech, a startup that sells technology to help financial firms better handle business errors, the companies said on Thursday.

titulo: Enel-BYD say Chile electric bus roll-out heralds more for region
resumo: Europe's biggest utility Enel and Chinese electric vehicle giant BYD on Thursday marked the arrival of 100 electric buses in Chile and said they would work together on similar plans for three other Latin American nations.

titulo: U.S. auto sales expected to dip in 2019, says NADA
resumo: New vehicle sales in the United States are expected to drop next year as higher interest rates and rising prices could prompt customers to hold off their car-buying plans, the National Automobile Dealers Association (NADA) said on Thursday.

titulo: WPP hires Publicis executive as chief marketing officer
resumo: WPP  said it had hired Laurent Ezekiel from its French rival Publicis  to be its chief marketing and growth officer, tasked with helping new boss Mark Read steer the world's biggest advertising group back to growth.

titulo: YouTube, under pressure for problem content, takes down 58 million videos in quarter

titulo: Deutsche Bank offices raided in money laundering probe
