titulo: Nissan-Renault-Mitsubishi alliance won't appoint interim chairman
resumo: Mitsubishi Motors'  Chief Executive Osamu Masuko said on Thursday that the company's three-way alliance with Nissan and Renault would not appoint an interim chairman and would shift instead to a system where the group would be led by the three CEOs.

titulo: Abercrombie & Fitch forecasts upbeat holiday quarter, shares soar
resumo: U.S. teen apparel retailer Abercrombie & Fitch Co  forecast same-store sales for the holiday quarter above analysts' estimates on Thursday, driven by strong sales at its Hollister and flagship stores, sending its shares soaring 24 percent in early trading.

titulo: Ford backs PM May's Brexit plan to avoid no-deal 'catastrophe'
resumo: Carmaker Ford backed Prime Minister Theresa May's Brexit deal on Thursday to avoid a no-deal "catastrophe," but said she still needed to guarantee long-term frictionless trade, which is key to the future of its plants in Britain.

titulo: Amazon picks New York City, Washington D.C. area for new offices
resumo: Amazon.com Inc  picked America's financial and political capitals for massive new offices on Tuesday, branching out from its home base in Seattle with plans to create more than 25,000 jobs in both New York City and an area just outside Washington, D.C.

titulo: Factbox: Amazon to get billions in breaks for HQ2 development
resumo: (The Factbox updated on Nov. 15 corrects to fix total New York City incentive figure to $2.81 billion from $1.525 billion.)

titulo: BMW mulls converting hybrids to electric only mode in polluted cities
resumo: BMW said it can force its hybrid cars switch to an electric-only mode in heavily polluted inner-city areas, helping its vehicles compete with pure battery-driven rivals as a raft of cities seek to create emissions-free zones.

titulo: Factbox: Unilever names beauty unit head Alan Jope as new CEO
resumo: Anglo-Dutch consumer goods company Unilever  said its CEO Paul Polman was retiring and would be replaced by the head of its beauty unit Alan Jope, 54, from January 1.

titulo: Ad buyers skeptical as Snap looks beyond teens for growth
resumo: Snap Inc  has pinned its hopes on the over-35 crowd to revive user growth for its photo messaging app, a shift ad buyers said could backfire.

titulo: Unilever CEO change not connected to HQ climbdown: chairman
resumo: The replacement of Unilever's CEO Paul Polman was not connected to a shareholder row earlier this year over a proposed move of the headquarters of the Anglo-Dutch consumer goods company from Britain to the Netherlands, said the group's chairman.

titulo: South Korea indicts group for leaking Samsung display tech to Chinese firm
resumo: South Korean prosecutors on Thursday said they have indicted nine people on suspicion of leaking Samsung Electronics Co Ltd flexible display technology to a Chinese company.

titulo: Supply chain reaction: trade war refugees race to relocate to Vietnam, Thailand
resumo: Fred Perrotta spent four years building a network of Chinese suppliers for his line of trendy backpacks, but as soon as the United States announced tariffs on almost half of its Chinese imports, he started looking for suppliers in other countries.

titulo: Tokyo prosecutors plan to seek extension of Ghosn's detention: Kyodo
resumo: Tokyo prosecutors plan to seek an extension of Carlos Ghosn's detention, after the ousted chairman of Nissan Motor Co  was arrested last week for alleged financial misconduct, Kyodo News reported on Thursday.

titulo: Indonesia's Go-Jek starts trial launch in Singapore, challenges Grab
resumo: Indonesian ride-hailing firm Go-Jek kicked off a trial launch in parts of Singapore on Thursday and plans to roll out an array of services through its app in early 2019, challenging dominant player Grab in the small city-state.

titulo: Trump administration to study tools to raise U.S. tariffs on Chinese autos
resumo: U.S. Trade Representative Robert Lighthizer said on Wednesday that he was examining all available tools to raise U.S. tariffs on Chinese vehicles to the 40 percent duties that China is now charging on U.S.-produced vehicles.

titulo: Japan central banker mum on alleged involvement in Ghosn transaction
resumo: A Bank of Japan board member declined on Thursday to comment on a media report that she was involved in an alleged attempt by former Nissan chairman Carlos Ghosn to shift personal investment losses to the automaker during her time at a commercial bank.

titulo: Huawei seeks clarification after New Zealand intelligence agency rejects 5G bid
resumo: Huawei Technologies Co Ltd said it was seeking clarification from New Zealand after the country's intelligence agency rejected the telecommunication industry's first request to use the Chinese firm's equipment in its planned 5G mobile network.

titulo: Volkswagen planning new North America factory for electric vehicles
resumo: Volkswagen  is deciding where to locate a new factory in North America to build electric vehicles for the U.S. market, the German automaker's new head for the Americas said on Wednesday.

titulo: Two U.S. pipelines rack up violations, threaten industry growth
resumo: Energy Transfer LP  and its Sunoco pipeline subsidiary have racked up more than 800 state and federal permit violations while racing to build two of the nation's largest natural gas pipelines, according to a Reuters analysis of government data and regulatory records.

titulo: Microsoft wins $479.2 million contract from U.S. Army
resumo: The U.S. Army said on Wednesday that it has awarded Microsoft Corp  a $479.2 million contract to supply prototypes for the Army's augmented reality systems helping soldiers in training and combat missions.

titulo: Altria in talks to take minority stake in Juul: sources
resumo: Tobacco company Altria Group Inc  is in talks to take a minority stake of 20 to 40 percent in e-cigarette maker Juul Labs Inc, people familiar with the matter said on Wednesday.

titulo: Exclusive: U.S. senators ask White House to probe ZTE work in Venezuela
resumo: Two U.S. senators on Wednesday asked the Trump administration to investigate whether Chinese telecommunications company ZTE Corp  violated U.S. sanctions by helping Venezuela set up a database that monitors the behavior of its citizens.

titulo: Tiffany sales to Chinese tourists disappoint, shares fall sharply
resumo: Tiffany & Co  on Wednesday reported quarterly sales that missed estimates as Chinese tourists spent less than expected at the jeweler's stores in the United States and Hong Kong, a shortfall that sent the company's shares down as much 13 percent.

titulo: Fed's Powell, in apparent dovish shift, says rates near neutral
resumo: U.S. Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell injected investors with a strong dose of optimism on Wednesday, saying that the central bank's policy rate is now "just below" estimates of a level that neither brakes nor boosts a healthy U.S. economy, comments that many investors read as signaling the Fed's three-year tightening cycle is drawing to a close.

titulo: Saudi Arabia inks deal for Lockheed's missile defense system
resumo: Saudi Arabia will buy Lockheed Martin's  $15 billion missile defense system, a U.S. Department of State spokesman said on Wednesday, after aggressive lobbying by the administration to close the deal that included a personal call between President Donald Trump and Saudi King Salman.

titulo: Wall Street jumps as Powell hints interest rate hikes may taper off
resumo: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell boosted U.S. stock markets on Wednesday when he said the policy rate is now "just below" estimates of a level that neither brakes nor boosts a healthy economy, comments that many took as signaling the Fed's three-year tightening cycle is drawing to a close.

titulo: Jewish Americans sue Airbnb over West Bank listing ban
resumo: A group of Jewish Americans sued Airbnb Inc on Wednesday in U.S. federal court, accusing the home rental company of religious discrimination over its decision last week to remove listings for about 200 homes in the Israeli-occupied West Bank.

titulo: Car market collapse outruns GM moves to keep up
resumo: General Motors Co's monumental announcement on Monday that it will close three car assembly plants in North America and slash its workforce will only partially close the gap between capacity and demand for the automaker's sedans, according to a Reuters analysis of industry production and capacity data.

titulo: Judge orders new talks in Fiat Chrysler diesel emissions case
resumo: A federal judge in San Francisco on Wednesday ordered the U.S. Justice Department and Fiat Chrysler Automobiles NV  to hold new talks with a court-appointed settlement master to try to settle the government's civil suit over the Italian-American automaker's diesel vehicle emissions.

titulo: Wall Street surges on hints of fewer rate hikes, dollar falls
resumo: Comments by Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell that benchmark U.S. interest rates were "just below" neutral sparked a sharp rally on Wall Street on Wednesday, easing investor worries about the pace of rate hikes by the U.S. central bank next year.

titulo: Apple's iPhone XR is company's best-selling model, executive says
resumo: Apple Inc's iPhone XR has been the company's best-selling iPhone model every day since it went on sale in mid-October, a company executive told Reuters on Wednesday.

titulo: Pimco buys all of $3 billion UniCredit bond: sources
resumo: U.S. fund Pacific Investment Management Co (Pimco) has bought all of a $3 billion, five-year bond offered with a hefty return by Italy's top bank UniCredit  to comply with capital buffer rules, two sources familiar with the matter said.

titulo: U.S. third-quarter growth unrevised, headwinds increasing
resumo: The U.S. economy slowed in the third quarter as previously reported, but the pace was likely strong enough to keep growth on track to hit the Trump administration's 3 percent target this year, even as momentum appears to have moderated further early in the fourth quarter.

titulo: Lampert's hedge fund and Cyrus Capital to team up for possible Sears bid: Bloomberg
resumo: Sears Holdings Corp  Chairman Eddie Lampert's hedge fund and Cyrus Capital Partners LP are preparing a potential takeover bid to keep the bankrupt retailer running, Bloomberg reported on Wednesday.

titulo: Microsoft's stock market value pulls ahead of Apple's
resumo: Microsoft Corp's  stock market value surpassed Apple Inc's , pulling ahead by as much as $3 billion on Wednesday as the Windows software maker benefited from optimism about demand for cloud computing services.

titulo: Detroit Three narrowing car production in North America
resumo: General Motors Co and fellow Detroit automakers Ford Motor Co and Fiat Chrysler Automobiles NV continue to winnow production of cars in North America as demand for traditional sedans wanes. GM announced on Monday it will halt production at three North American assembly plants and slash thousands of jobs.

titulo: Trump retweets item saying GM should pay back bailout money
resumo: President Donald Trump on Wednesday retweeted a suggestion that General Motors Co should pay back the money the government spent to keep it afloat as part of the auto industry bailout if it doesn't keep jobs in the country.

titulo: Ford reshuffles U.S. plants to beef up SUV, truck production
resumo: Ford Motor Co  said on Wednesday it will reshuffle workers at several of its plants to meet rising demand for pickup trucks and large SUVs, a process that will require finding new positions for 150 workers displaced by the changes but not result in job cuts.

titulo: Instant View: Fed's gradual rate hikes balance against risks - Powell
resumo: U.S. Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell said on Wednesday that while there was "a great deal to like" about U.S. prospects, the Fed's gradual interest-rate hikes are meant to balance risks as it tries to keep the economy on track.

titulo: State-owned Israel Aerospace to partner with Boeing on potential aviation contracts
resumo: State-owned Israel Aerospace Industries (IAI) said on Wednesday it signed an agreement with U.S. planemaker Boeing Co  last week to cooperate on commercial and military aviation in Israel and some other markets.

titulo: Fed's Powell: Financial risks 'moderate' despite vulnerabilities
resumo: The U.S. faces only moderate financial risks despite elevated asset prices and concern over the possible impact of rising corporate debt, U.S. Fed chairman Jerome Powell said on Wednesday as the central bank released a broad overview of the health of credit markets and the financial system.

titulo: Danske Bank charged over alleged violations of money laundering law
resumo: Denmark's state prosecutor has filed preliminary charges against Danske Bank  for alleged violations of the country's anti-money laundering act in relation to its Estonian branch.

titulo: Insurer AXA raises profit targets in wake of $15 billion XL deal
resumo: AXA , Europe's second-biggest insurer, expects its profitability to improve in the next two years thanks to U.S. business brought in by its new XL arm, and said it would now focus on expanding in Asia.

titulo: U.S. new home sales drop to more than 2-1/2-year low
resumo: Sales of new U.S. single-family homes tumbled to a more than 2-1/2-year low in October amid sharp declines in all four regions, further evidence that higher mortgage rates were hurting the housing market.

titulo: BMW chief says considering second U.S. manufacturing plant

titulo: Wall Street grows cautious but still optimistic: Reuters poll
resumo: Fears of a prolonged U.S.-China trade war and a potentially overzealous Federal Reserve have left Wall Street strategists less optimistic about stock market gains next year, but they still expect a decent increase, a Reuters poll found.

titulo: Daimler to start making electric cars in China in 2019
resumo: Daimler  will start building electric cars in China next year as a way to meet Beijing's stringent anti-pollution quotas for carmakers, even as demand for conventional models like the Mercedes-Benz Maybach remains strong.

titulo: NBA strikes betting data deals with Sportradar, Genius Sports
resumo: The National Basketball Association (NBA) struck its first betting data partnerships, allowing Genius Sports and Sportradar AG to distribute data to licensed sports betting operators in the United States.

titulo: Siemens CEO received pay rise in 2018: annual report
resumo: Siemens paid its chief executive Joe Kaeser a total of 7.82 million euros ($8.82 million) during its 2018 financial year, the German engineering company said in its annual report on Wednesday, up from 7.65 million euros a year earlier.

titulo: Bitcoin up 10 percent, on course for its biggest daily rise since April
resumo: Bitcoin rallied 10 percent on Wednesday to more than $4,100, putting it on course for its biggest daily rise since mid-April as it pulled itself out of a recent slump.

titulo: Seeds of Renault-Nissan crisis sown in Macron's 'raid'
resumo: The arrest of Renault-Nissan boss Carlos Ghosn has triggered new attempts by the Japanese carmaker to shake off the control of its French parent - adding to the problems piling up on President Emmanuel Macron's desk in the Elysee Palace.

titulo: Dismantling the oil industry: rough North Sea waters test new ideas
resumo: (This Nov. 27 story has been refiled to correct spelling of surname of Oil and Gas UK decommissioning manager.)

titulo: Deutsche Bank Americas head expected to leave: sources
resumo: Deutsche Bank's  head of the Americas, Tom Patrick, is likely to leave the bank, possibly by the end of the year, two people with knowledge of the matter said on Wednesday.

titulo: Bangladesh factory safety group seeks to delay closure, flags lingering risks
resumo: A group set up by European fashion brands to improve factory safety in Bangladesh has sought a stay on a court order that calls for its local operations to end this week, its executive director said on Wednesday.

titulo: Bitcoin gains 6 percent as it heads for best day since July
resumo: Bitcoin bounced more than 6 percent on Wednesday, climbing above $4,000 and heading for its biggest daily jump since July as it clawed back ground after recent heavy selling.

titulo: Cryptocurrencies to survive sell-off - Allianz's El-Erian
resumo: Cryptocurrencies are here to stay despite a prolonged slump this year, and will gain wider acceptance after the recent entry of more institutional investors in the space, Mohamed El-Erian, chief economic adviser at Allianz, said on Tuesday.

titulo: Exclusive: Russia plans stiffer fines for tech firms that break rules - sources
resumo: Russia plans to impose stiffer fines on technology firms that fail to comply with Russian laws, sources familiar with the plans said, raising the stakes in the Kremlin's fight with global tech giants such as Facebook  and Google.

titulo: New Zealand rejects Huawei's first 5G bid citing national security risk
resumo: New Zealand's intelligence agency has rejected the telecom industry's first request in the country to use 5G equipment provided by China's Huawei Technologies Co Ltd, citing concerns about national security.

titulo: 'Out of control': Chinese authorities slam ride-hailing giant Didi over safety
resumo: Chinese authorities announced a broad crackdown on China's ride-hailing industry on Wednesday, targeting market-leader Didi Chuxing with fines following the deaths of two passengers in separate incidents earlier this year.

titulo: Auditor had questioned Nissan on payments to Ghosn at heart of scandal: source
resumo: Nissan Motor Co’s  auditor had repeatedly questioned transactions at the heart of allegations of financial misconduct by former chief Carlos Ghosn but Nissan said they were proper, a person with direct knowledge of the matter said on Wednesday.

titulo: Exclusive: Brazil oil tanker collision reveals offshore regulatory gaps
resumo: Brazil has more than doubled the number of risky ship-to-ship oil transfers this year, but its monitoring of such offshore maneuvers is lax, to a point where a July 2017 collision between two tankers was not reported, according to a Reuters review of government and shipping records.

titulo: Trump threatens to cut GM subsidies in retaliation for U.S. job cuts
resumo: U.S. President Donald Trump threatened on Tuesday to eliminate subsidies for General Motors Co in retaliation for the automaker cutting U.S. jobs and plants, and the automaker also took fire from Canadian political and labor leaders for cutbacks there.

titulo: U.S. court revives Mattress Firm defamation case against Sleep Number
resumo: A federal appeals court on Tuesday revived for a second time a 11-year-old defamation lawsuit by bedding retailer Mattress Firm Inc against adjustable bed maker Sleep Number Corp .

titulo: Single word keeps some farmers from getting Trump's aid to offset tariffs
resumo: A Republican lawmaker wants to change a single word in the Trump administration's farm aid program saying some soybean growers in Louisiana cannot qualify for the payments designed to offset farmers' losses from tariffs against China.

titulo: Trump says 'not even a little bit happy' with Fed's Powell: report
resumo: U.S. President Donald Trump on Tuesday kept up his criticism of Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell, saying rising interest rates and other Fed policies were damaging the U.S. economy, the Washington Post said.

titulo: Sears secures court approval for additional $350 million loan
resumo: Sears Holdings Corp  won court approval on Tuesday for $350 million in critical bankruptcy financing that will keep the 125-year-old retailer operating through the holidays while it attempts to reorganize.

titulo: Hong Kong's finance sector M&A deals fall prey to China crackdown
resumo: Mergers and acquisitions targeting Hong Kong financial firms have slumped this year, with insurance sector deals particularly hard hit, as China's moves to tighten capital controls and crack down on corporate debt have driven mainland buyers away.

titulo: Amazon adds antenna service for satellite data; courts space industry
resumo: Amazon.com Inc  said on Tuesday it was starting a new antenna service for uploading and downloading data from satellites, in a bid to lure space industry and other customers to the cloud.

titulo: Wall Street reverses losses after White House adviser's trade remarks

titulo: Oshkosh wins $1.7 billion U.S. defense contract: Pentagon
resumo: Oshkosh Corp  has been awarded a $1.7 billion modification to a U.S. defense contract to exercise options for 6,107 vehicles and 22,166 kits, the Pentagon said on Tuesday.

titulo: Workers return to GM's Canada plant; union to meet Trudeau

titulo: Exclusive: China not seriously considering U.S. Treasuries as trade war weapon - envoy

titulo: Oil prices edge lower ahead of G20, OPEC meetings
resumo: Oil prices dipped on Tuesday, weighed down by uncertainty over the U.S.-China trade war and signs of increased global crude production, but losses were limited by expectations that crude exporters would agree to cut output at an upcoming OPEC meeting.

titulo: Deutsche Bank mulls shake-up of high-level executives: WSJ
resumo: Deutsche Bank AG  is planning a high-level shake up that could result in the departure of top executives central to its relationships with key regulators in the United States and Europe, the Wall Street Journal reported on Tuesday.

titulo: World stocks finish higher on China trade-deal hopes; dollar rises
resumo: Stocks on Wall Street logged modest gains on Tuesday as traders remained hopeful that the United States and China can work toward resolving their trade-related differences at the upcoming G20 Summit.

titulo: Disney, Fox deny claims in $1 billion Malaysia theme park lawsuit
resumo: Walt Disney Co  and Twenty-First Century Fox Inc  on Tuesday rejected claims in a Malaysia casino operator's lawsuit accusing them of abandoning a licensing contract tied to the planned construction of a Fox-branded theme park.

titulo: TransCanada asks Montana court to allow preliminary work on Keystone XL

titulo: Former Facebook worker says firm not doing enough to deal with racial discrimination

titulo: Trump open to deal with Xi at dinner but with conditions: Kudlow

titulo: Fed official frets over risks to Apple Pay, other mobile payments

titulo: Opening on Thanksgiving hurts retailers more than it helps
resumo: As store sales fall and purchases shift online over the Thanksgiving weekend, many U.S. retailers still open their doors on Thanksgiving itself, Thursday evening - to their detriment.

titulo: United Technologies to separate into three companies
resumo: United Technologies Corp  said on Monday it would separate into three companies consisting of its aerospace, elevators and building divisions, making it the latest industrial conglomerate to pursue such a break-up.

titulo: Trump is right, jobs for black Americans abound. Here's why it may not last
resumo: Ask Memphis residents and they might say that President Donald Trump got this one right: this is the best job market as far as many in this majority black city can remember.

titulo: White House adviser says China-U.S. trade deal still possible
resumo: White House economic adviser Larry Kudlow said on Tuesday that U.S. President Donald Trump will have a dinner meeting with Chinese President Xi Jinping at the upcoming G20 gathering in Argentina and also held open the possibility that the two countries would reach a trade deal.

titulo: Milan prosecutors wrap up Gucci tax probe, trial likely: source
resumo: Milan prosecutors have wrapped up their probe into alleged tax evasion of around 1 billion euros ($1.13 billion) by fashion group Gucci, paving the way for a formal request for a trial, a judicial source said on Tuesday.

titulo: Fed urged to get more serious about U.S. corporate debt risks
resumo: Bankers, executives and investors are warning Federal Reserve officials behind closed doors that record leveraged lending to companies from lightly-regulated corners of Wall Street could make any economic downturn harder to manage.

titulo: Fewer Americans shopped during Thanksgiving weekend in 2018
resumo: More than 165 million U.S. shoppers made purchases over the five-day Thanksgiving weekend, the National Retail Federation said on Tuesday, beating the industry group's expectations this year but lower than last year.

titulo: Exclusive: EPA will not reallocate waived biofuel volumes to 2019 - official
resumo: The U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) has rejected requests from the corn lobby to reallocate biofuel volumes waived under its small refinery exemption program into its 2019 mandate, an agency official told Reuters on Tuesday.

titulo: Auto group says Tesla's China sales plunge; company's shares slide
resumo: Tesla Inc's  vehicle sales in China sank 70 percent last month from a year ago, the country's passenger car association told Reuters on Tuesday, underscoring how the Sino-U.S. trade war is hurting the U.S. electric carmaker.

resumo: Chinese steel producers ran up losses for the first time in three years this month as prices slid into a bear market on weak demand and near-record supply, ending years of solid profit margins.

titulo: Microsoft's stock market value catches up with Apple

resumo: Russian businessman Vladimir Potanin plans to appeal a London court decision to block him from buying shares in mining company Norilsk Nickel  from fellow investor Roman Abramovich, representatives at Potanin's investment firm said on Tuesday.

resumo: (The Nov. 26 story corrects source of quote in eleventh paragraph to Bill Bullard, the CEO of R-CALF USA, not U.S. Meat Export Federation spokesman Joe Schuele)

titulo: Trump may impose tariffs on imported cars from next week: report

titulo: Fed's Clarida more sensitive to data, still backs U.S. rate hikes

resumo: (The Nov. 26 story corrects paragraphs 4 and 5 to show the SEC filed charges against Musk and Tesla, and that Musk and Tesla settled those charges without admitting or denying the SEC's allegations)

resumo: A senior executive at Mitsubishi Motors Corp  said on Tuesday its alliance with Nissan Motor  and Renault SA  can survive management upheaval, a day after it fired Carlos Ghosn as chairman over financial misconduct allegations.

titulo: Fed's Bullard: 'cracks' in growth may shape Fed 2019 debate
resumo: For nearly two years the U.S. Federal Reserve has pushed ahead with steady rate increases in an economy that has done better than expected, boosted by government spending, tax cuts, and global growth that made the Fed's policy choices seem almost a footnote.

titulo: Special Report: Little known to many investors, cryptocurrency reviews are for sale
resumo: When cryptocurrency issuers want positive coverage for their virtual coins, they buy it.

titulo: Fed wants smallest possible portfolio, says Vice Chair Clarida
resumo: The U.S. Federal Reserve ultimately wants to have the smallest balance sheet possible while still being able to control its policy rate, Fed Vice Chair Richard Clarida said on Tuesday.

titulo: Alibaba's Jack Ma is a Communist Party member, China state paper reveals
resumo: Jack Ma, the head of e-commerce giant Alibaba Group Holding Ltd and China's best-known capitalist, is a Communist Party member, the official Party newspaper said on Monday, debunking a public assumption the billionaire was politically unattached.

titulo: EU trade chief urges U.S. to join talks on WTO reform
resumo: The European Union called on Washington on Tuesday to start talking over ways of reforming the World Trade Organization to prevent a paralysis of the international body.

titulo: H&M to close down its struggling Cheap Monday brand
resumo: H&M , the world's second-biggest fashion retailer, said on Tuesday it would close down its struggling independent brand Cheap Monday.

titulo: Lufthansa sees three Europe carriers in global 'champions league'
resumo: The chief executive of Germany's Lufthansa  said he expects the company to take part in more consolidation in the industry that will eventually leave three global carriers in Europe.

titulo: DBS to almost double staff, triple revenue of Mideast private banking

titulo: Trump says he expects to raise China tariffs: Wall Street Journal

titulo: British and Dutch regulators fine Uber for 2016 data hack
resumo: British and Dutch regulators on Tuesday fined ride-hailing service Uber [UBER.UL] for failing to protect customers' personal information during a 2016 cyber attack involving millions of users.

titulo: Mitsubishi Motors executive stresses importance of alliance with Nissan, Renault

titulo: Airbnb hires Amazon veteran as CFO ahead of potential IPO
resumo: Home-renting company Airbnb Inc on Monday hired Amazon.com Inc  veteran Dave Stephenson as chief financial officer (CFO), ahead of a widely anticipated initial public offering next year.

resumo: General Motors Co said on Monday it will cut production of slow-selling models and slash its North American workforce because of a declining market for traditional gas-powered sedans, shifting more investment to electric and autonomous vehicles.

titulo: With risky bets back in play, stock selloff revives 'Volmageddon' memories

resumo: Sandy Hetrick drove 15 miles from her home to a Sears in Media, Pennsylvania on Wednesday to buy folding chairs and clothes.

resumo: Tesla Inc  has produced more than 11,500 cars, including about 7,400 Model 3s, so far this month, automotive news website Electrek reported on Friday, citing a source familiar with the matter.

resumo: Citigroup Inc  reported a better-than-expected quarterly profit on Friday, helped by lower expenses, higher bond trading revenue and strength in its consumer banking business in Mexico.

titulo: Trump's ethanol plan: Hype now, legal fights later

titulo: Cokes, smokes and clicks: How Oxxo corner stores are cashing in on Mexican e-commerce

titulo: Take Five: World markets themes for the week ahead
resumo: Following are five big themes likely to dominate thinking of investors and traders in the coming week and the Reuters stories related to them.

titulo: Toyota, SoftBank in first-ever alliance, target self-driving car services
resumo: (This October 4 story corrects paragraph 17 to show Toyota has partnership, not investment, in Didi Chuxing.)

titulo: New U.S. tariff threat on Chinese goods hits stocks, dollar
resumo: A gauge of global equity markets eased and the dollar slipped on Monday as an expected new round of tariffs from Washington on Chinese goods made investors skittish about an escalation of a  U.S.-Sino dispute over trade.

titulo: Trump says he will announce decision on China tariffs after market close
resumo: U.S. President Donald Trump said on Monday he would announce his latest plan for China tariffs after the markets close, with expectations he would levy about $200 billion worth of Chinese imports.

titulo: Saudi's PIF invests more than $1 billion in electric carmaker Lucid Motors
resumo: Saudi Arabia's Public Investment Fund (PIF) has agreed to invest more than $1 billion in Lucid Motors, adding to the emerging competition facing U.S. electric vehicle maker Tesla Inc .

titulo: U.S. review of CVS deal for Aetna may end soon: source
resumo: The U.S. Justice Department's antitrust review of CVS Health Corp's  planned purchase of Aetna Inc  may conclude soon, a source familiar with the review said on Monday.

titulo: Trump economic adviser says U.S. wants serious trade talks with China
resumo: The United States is ready to negotiate a trade deal with China whenever Beijing is prepared for serious talks that will reduce tariffs and eliminate non-tariff trade barriers, top White House economic adviser Larry Kudlow said on Monday.

titulo: Oil near flat as market weighs U.S.-China trade tensions, Iran sanctions
resumo: Oil prices were little changed on Monday as the market weighed deepening trade tension between the U.S. and China that is expected to dent global crude demand and potential supply tightening due to Iran sanctions.

titulo: Trump adviser eyes entitlement cuts to plug U.S. budget gaps
resumo: A top economic adviser to President Donald Trump said on Monday he expects U.S. budget deficits of about 4 percent to 5 percent of the country's economic output for the next one to two years, adding that there would likely be an effort in 2019 to cut spending on entitlement programs.

titulo: Goldman names Tim O'Neill as vice-chairman: memo
resumo: Goldman Sachs Group Inc  named Tim O'Neill as its vice-chairman, according to an internal memo seen by Reuters, the latest top-level appointment ahead of David Solomon taking charge as chief executive officer of the Wall Street bank.

titulo: Hearings in Volkswagen investor lawsuit on hold until end-November
resumo: Investors suing Volkswagen  for billions in compensation from the hit to the carmaker's share price from its diesel emissions scandal will have to wait until end-November for court hearings to continue, a German judge ruled on Monday.

titulo: Qualcomm, Apple kick off second trial seeking iPhone import ban
resumo: A second trial between Apple Inc  and Qualcomm Inc  before the U.S. International Trade Commission began Monday in Washington, D.C., with the iPhone maker squaring off against the world's biggest mobile chipmaker over whether Apple should be banned from importing several recent iPhone models to the United States.

titulo: Novartis links bonuses to ethics in bid to rebuild reputation
resumo: Swiss drugmaker Novartis  has revealed its employees only get a bonus if they meet or exceed expectations for ethical behavior as it seeks to address past shortcomings that have damaged its reputation.

titulo: Fox says Sky shareholders have until October 6 to accept offer
resumo: Twenty-First Century Fox  said on Monday that its 14 pounds per share bid for Sky Plc  will remain open for acceptances until October 6.

titulo: UPS to hire 100,000 for holiday season, up 5 percent from last year
resumo: United Parcel Service Inc would hire about 100,000 temporary employees for the crucial holiday season, up about 5 percent from a year earlier, the world's biggest package delivery company said on Monday.

titulo: Activist ValueAct raises stake in Horizon Discovery
resumo: Activist hedge fund ValueAct Capital has nearly doubled its stake in Horizon Discovery Group , buying an additional 7.5 million shares from Woodford Investment Management.

titulo: Amazon investigating claims of employees leaking data for bribes
resumo: Amazon.com Inc  said on Monday it was investigating suspected internal leaks of confidential information by its employees for bribes to remove fake reviews and other seller scams from its website.

titulo: JD.com CEO, under investigation for rape allegation, skips China forum
resumo: The CEO of Chinese e-commerce giant JD.com , Richard Liu, will no longer appear at a high-profile state-run tech forum in Shanghai this week that he had earlier been scheduled to attend.

titulo: Musk says Tesla now in 'delivery logistics hell'
resumo: The focus on Tesla Inc's  battle to ramp up production swung to concerns over logistics and distribution on Monday, after Chief Executive Officer Elon Musk acknowledged there were delivery bottlenecks and promised to swiftly iron out the delays.

titulo: DowDuPont CEO Breen takes executive chair role in new DuPont
resumo: DowDuPont  Chief Executive Edward Breen will oversee the specialty-products unit that carries most of the company's legacy DuPont operations after the chemicals major splits back into three different businesses, the company said on Monday.

titulo: Citigroup hires three software investment bankers
resumo: Citigroup Inc  has hired three investment bankers for its technology investment banking group focused on software in the United States and in Europe, according to a memo published internally at the bank and confirmed by a spokeswoman on Monday.

titulo: Ferrari investors want assurance on targets and SUV plans
resumo: Ferrari's  new boss has his work cut out on Tuesday to convince investors that the supercar maker can hit mid-term targets he described last month as "aspirational".

titulo: EU regulators to rule on Disney's $71 billion bid for Fox assets by October 19
resumo: EU antitrust regulators have set an Oct. 19 deadline for their ruling on Walt Disney's  $71.3 billion bid for Twenty-First Century Fox Inc's  entertainment assets, the European Commission said on Monday.

titulo: How JPMorgan's CFO became the top prospect to succeed Dimon
resumo: As a single mother with a British accent who loves numbers, Marianne Lake bears little resemblance to Jamie Dimon, the longtime chief executive of JPMorgan Chase & Co , known for his bravado, quips and occasional profanity-laced outbursts.

titulo: EU antitrust ruling on Microsoft buy of GitHub due by October 19
resumo: EU antitrust regulators will decide by Oct. 19 whether to clear U.S. software giant Microsoft's $7.5 billion acquisition of privately held coding website GitHub.

titulo: As its trading debut looms, China's Meituan locked in battle of super-apps
resumo: (This version of the story corrects $100 million to $100 billion in 13th paragraph)

titulo: High U.S. stock valuations hinge on inflation, interest rates
resumo: Investors are banking on tame inflation and interest rates to support U.S. stock prices and help counter any concerns over an anticipated slowdown in corporate earnings growth next year.

titulo: Take Five: World markets themes for the week ahead
resumo: Following are five big themes likely to dominate the thinking of investors and traders in the coming week, and the Reuters stories related to them.

titulo: Tesla to bring most collision repairs in-house: Musk
resumo: Tesla Inc is bringing most collision repairs in-house, Chief Executive Officer Elon Musk said in a tweet on Sunday, saying owners of the vehicles were experiencing excessive wait times at body shops.

titulo: Meredith Corp to sell Time media brand to Marc and Lynne Benioff
resumo: Meredith Corp  said on Sunday it entered into a definitive agreement to sell the Time media brand to Marc and Lynne Benioff for $190 million in cash.

titulo: Fire contained at Tesla Gigafactory in Nevada
resumo: A fire broke out at Tesla Inc's  Gigafactory in Nevada's Tahoe-Reno Industrial Center late on Saturday but was contained by early Sunday and production had resumed, emergency officials and the company said.

titulo: Deutsche to shift more assets to Frankfurt, ringfence UK operations after Brexit: source
resumo: Deutsche Bank  is considering shifting large volumes of assets from London to Frankfurt after the UK's planned exit from the European Union next year to meet demands from European regulators, a person close to the matter said on Sunday.

titulo: Carmakers must offer customers incentives to replace diesel models: German minister
resumo: Germany's transport minister is in talks with carmakers about how much money they should offer customers to exchange old diesel models for new cars, Frankfurter Allgemeine Zeitung reported.

titulo: China may reject new trade talks if more tariffs imposed: WSJ
resumo: The Chinese government may decline to participate in proposed trade talks with the United States later this month if the Trump administration moves forward with additional tariffs on imported Chinese goods, the Wall Street Journal reported on Sunday, citing Chinese officials.

titulo: 'Illusion' to think states can completely prevent financial crises: Weidmann
resumo: Governments cannot completely prevent a repeat of events like the 2008 global financial crisis even though regulations have been tightened since the collapse of Lehman Brothers a decade ago, Germany's top central banker told Bild newspaper.

titulo: Tariffs threaten market-leading tech, consumer stocks
resumo: U.S. technology and consumer discretionary stocks have been insulated from global trade tensions, but if another round of U.S. tariffs on Chinese goods goes into effect, even those high-flying sectors could come down to earth.

titulo: JP Morgan chief Dimon says shouldn't have made remarks about Trump
resumo: JPMorgan Chase & Co Chief Executive Jamie Dimon on Sunday said he regretted comments that he was "smarter" than U.S. President Donald Trump and could beat him in an election.

titulo: Trump 'likely' to announce new China tariffs as early as Monday: source
resumo: U.S. President Donald Trump is likely to announce new tariffs on about $200 billion on Chinese imports as early as Monday, a senior administration official told Reuters on Saturday.

titulo: Exclusive: Saudi Arabia to auction detained tycoon's real estate assets - sources
resumo: Saudi Arabia will auction real estate owned by indebted billionaire Maan al-Sanea and his company starting next month to help repay billions of riyals due to creditors, sources familiar with the matter told Reuters.

titulo: BMW to recall more than 139,000 of its 3-series cars in China from November
resumo: German automaker BMW  will recall more than 139,000 of its 3-series cars in China from Nov. 9, because of a defect in the air-conditioning system, the market regulator said.

titulo: BMW drives to cut battery costs, share costs on autonomous vehicles: executive
resumo: German luxury vehicle maker BMW AG  plans more deals with mining companies to secure electric vehicle battery materials, and is open to forming alliances to share the costs of developing autonomous-vehicle systems, the automaker's research and development chief told Reuters.

titulo: Credit Suisse CEO targets annual profit of 5-6 billion Swiss francs: newspaper
resumo: Credit Suisse  is aiming for an annual profit of 5 to 6 billion Swiss francs for the next two years as the bank puts its problems behind it, Chief Executive Tidjane Thiam told Swiss newspaper NZZ am Sonntag in an interview to be published on Sunday.

titulo: Iran says Saudi Arabia and Russia have taken oil market 'hostage': SHANA
resumo: Iran's OPEC governor said on Saturday that Saudi Arabia and Russia have taken the oil market "hostage" as U.S. President Donald Trump seeks to impose fresh sanctions on Iranian oil sales.

titulo: Campbell, Third Point urge shareholders to vote for two different boards
resumo: Campbell Soup Co  and hedge fund Third Point LLC on Friday filed preliminary proxy materials urging the food company's shareholders to vote in favor of two entirely different slates of board nominees.

titulo: Brazil space station open for small satellite business
resumo: Brazil is ready to launch small commercial rockets from its space base near the equator as soon as it agrees to safeguard U.S. technology that is dominant in the industry, the Brazilian Air Force officer managing the space program said on Friday.

titulo: Oil mixed as China tariff talk scotches early rally
resumo: Oil prices pulled back on Friday on concerns additional U.S. tariffs would be placed on Chinese imports, after an earlier rally triggered by worries that more sanctions on Iran might constrict supply.

titulo: Lockheed wins contract for U.S. Air Force GPS satellites
resumo: The U.S. Air Force said on Friday it had chosen Lockheed Martin  to build 22 next-generation Global Positioning System satellites worth up to $7.2 billion, part of a major effort to modernize the GPS constellation of satellites.

titulo: Trump readies tariffs on $200 billion more Chinese goods despite talks: source
resumo: U.S. President Donald Trump has directed aides to proceed with tariffs on about another $200 billion of Chinese goods, despite Treasury Secretary Steven Mnuchin's attempts to restart trade talks with China, a source familiar with the matter said on Friday.

titulo: Volkswagen to end production of the Beetle next year
resumo: Volkswagen  said on Thursday it would stop producing its Beetle compact car in 2019, ending a model that looked backward to the 1960s counterculture as the automaker prepares for a leap toward a future of mass-market electric cars.

titulo: Volvo Cars seeks U.S. tariff exemption for Chinese-made SUV
resumo: Volvo Cars has asked the Trump administration to exempt the company's Chinese-made mid-size XC60 SUV from new 25 percent tariffs, the latest automaker to seek relief from new levies on imports from China.

titulo: Trade conflict looms but higher rates, oil prices help some stocks
resumo: A world stock index climbed as bank and energy shares rose on Friday, but the momentum was sapped by a report that Washington may fire a new salvo in its trade war with Beijing.

titulo: Wall Street flat as looming tariffs offset gains in financials
resumo: U.S. stocks ended little changed on Friday as financials  gained with bond yields, while news that President Donald Trump instructed aides to proceed with tariffs on about $200 billion more of Chinese products limited gains.

titulo: Yellen: Fed should commit to future 'booms' to make up for major busts
resumo: The U.S. Federal Reserve should commit to letting economic booms run on enough to fully offset collapses like the 2007 to 2009 Great Recession, former Fed chair Janet Yellen said on Friday, urging the central bank to make "lower-for-longer" its official motto for interest rates following serious downturns.

titulo: Fed faces 'quite challenging' period keeping recovery on track: Yellen
resumo: The Federal Reserve faces a "quite challenging" period in trying to keep the U.S. recovery on track amid a risk that low unemployment could lead to an overheated economy, former Fed chief Janet Yellen said on Friday.

titulo: Daly appointment shows diversity gap between regional Feds
resumo: The appointment of Mary Daly as the second-ever woman to head up the San Francisco Federal Reserve makes plain that some regional central banks continue to make strides in creating a diverse leadership as criticism intensifies about the Fed's hiring policies while others fall behind.

titulo: Mary Daly, labor expert, picked to run San Francisco Fed
resumo: Mary Daly, a labor economist who dropped out of high school, later earned a PhD from Syracuse University, joined the San Francisco Fed's research department in 1996 and led it since 2017, was picked on Friday to be the bank's next president after a five-month search.

titulo: U.S. House Democratic leader: NAFTA should stay a trilateral deal
resumo: U.S. House of Representatives Democratic Leader Nancy Pelosi on Friday warned the Trump administration that NAFTA should be maintained as a trilateral pact between the United States, Mexico and Canada and not just as a U.S.-Mexico arrangement.

titulo: Allergan says revenue from aesthetics unit may double by 2025
resumo: Allergan Plc  said revenue from its medical aesthetics business could double by 2025, even as it faces increasing competition for its blockbuster wrinkle treatment, Botox.

titulo: The home of Fiat says 'ciao' to Sergio Marchionne
resumo: The home of Fiat bid farewell on Friday to Sergio Marchionne, one of the auto industry's most exacting and determined leaders, who rescued the Italian car giant and U.S. Chrysler.

titulo: U.S. union urges states to look into T-Mobile purchase of Sprint
resumo: The Communication Workers of America labor union, which opposes T-Mobile's proposed purchase of rival Sprint, has written to all 50 state attorneys general to highlight potential job losses from the proposed deal as well as antitrust concerns.

titulo: Britain's FCA bans ex-Deutsche Bank trader after Euribor rigging
resumo: Britain's Financial Conduct Authority has banned former Deutsche Bank trader Christian Bittar from performing any function related to any regulated financial activity, the financial watchdog said on Friday.

titulo: Borrowers branch out
resumo: LONDON (IFR) - Blue-chip corporates including Nestle are seeking to diversify their funding to avoid exposure to individual market risks.

titulo: U.S. retail sales slow, but exhibit underlying strength
resumo: U.S. retail sales recorded their smallest gain in six months in August as consumers cut back on purchases of motor vehicles and clothing, but upward revisions to July data kept intact expectations of strong economic growth in the third quarter.

titulo: Airbus says worst-case Brexit would hit its sites beyond BritainL Spiegel
resumo: A worst-case scenario Brexit would affect Airbus's sites not only in Britain but also in the rest of Europe, board member Tom Williams has told the German magazine Der Spiegel.

titulo: Citigroup pays $12 million to settle dark pool probe
resumo: Citigroup Inc on Friday was ordered to pay more than $12 million by U.S. regulators after it was found that the bank's investment banking and financial advisory unit misled users of a "dark pool" operated by one of its affiliates.

titulo: Metro puts struggling Real hypermarket chain on the block
resumo: German retailer Metro AG plans to sell its struggling Real hypermarkets and is confident of interest in the chain, though bankers played down talk it might attract Amazon and a price tag of up to 1 billion euros ($1.2 billion).

titulo: U.S. House Democratic leader says NAFTA should remain trilateral
resumo: U.S. House of Representatives Democratic Leader Nancy Pelosi on Friday warned the Trump administration that the NAFTA trade agreement should be maintained as a trilateral pact between the United States, Mexico and Canada and not just a U.S.-Mexico arrangement.

titulo: L Brands to close all Henri Bendel stores, website
resumo: Victoria's Secret owner L Brands Inc  on Thursday said it would close all 23 of its Henri Bendel stores and the Henri Bendel e-commerce website in January.

titulo: Fed policy to turn mildly restrictive in 2019, Evans says
resumo: The Federal Reserve's interest-rate hikes will begin to drag on U.S. economic growth and employment by next year, a U.S. central banker said Friday, as the level of borrowing costs appropriately turns "mildly restrictive" after more than a decade of acting as a stimulant.

titulo: Fed's Evans says premature to read much into yield curve
resumo: A narrowing gap between long-term and short-term borrowing costs does not necessarily signal a coming recession, Federal Reserve Bank of Chicago President Charles Evans said on Friday.

titulo: Fed's Evans sees one or two more rate hikes this year
resumo: Federal Reserve Bank of Chicago President Charles Evans said on Friday that two further interest-rate hikes this year by the U.S. central bank would not be surprising, given the strength of the economy.

titulo: U.S. imposes sanctions on Thai company it accuses of aiding Iran
resumo: The United States on Friday imposed sanctions against a Thai aviation company that it said was acting on behalf of Iran's Mahan Air, which it accused of ferrying troops and supplies into Syria in support of Syrian President Bashar al-Assad.

titulo: UK public pension group to oppose Ryanair at AGM
resumo: Ryanair faced growing criticism from unions and investors on Friday about how it has handled an industrial relations revolt ahead of its annual shareholder meeting next week when its veteran chairman's position will be challenged.

titulo: Brexit endgame nears as series of summits begins
resumo: Brexit talks are set to intensify over the coming week, with the first of three summits that European Union leaders hope will settle a divorce deal for departing Britain within the next two months.

titulo: Amazon's Bezos says Trump should be 'glad' of media scrutiny
resumo: Amazon.com Inc  Chief Executive Jeff Bezos said on Thursday the company would announce a decision on where it will build a second headquarters before the end of the year, and also said that President Donald Trump is wrong to "attack the media."

titulo: U.S. health secretary backs proposed FDA crackdown on e-cigarettes: CNBC
resumo: U.S. Health and Human Services Secretary Alex Azar told CNBC on Friday he was "completely in support" of the Food and Drug Administration's proposed crackdown on e-cigarettes.

titulo: U.S. business inventories increase solidly in July
resumo: U.S. business inventories rose solidly in July, boosted by a larger than initially estimated increase in the stock of motor vehicles.

titulo: China to ask Wall Street for ideas on improving U.S. ties
resumo: China will ask Wall Street firms for ways to improve ties with the United States and suggestions to open up its financial sector at a day-long meeting in Beijing on Sunday, people familiar with the matter said.

titulo: China's export-dependent provinces scramble for shelter from U.S. trade storm
resumo: China's export-dependent cities and provinces are scrambling to provide relief to exporters, stabilize employment and avert the possibility of social unrest as an intensifying trade dispute with the United States threatens to further erode business.

titulo: U.S. auto sector output boosts industrial production
resumo: U.S. industrial production rose in August, as strong output in auto manufacturing offset lackluster production in the rest of the factory sector.

titulo: U.S. import prices post largest drop in more than one-and-a-half years
resumo: U.S. import prices recorded their biggest drop in more than 1-1/2 years in August amid declines in the cost of fuels and a range of other goods, suggesting a strong dollar was curbing imported inflation pressures.

titulo: U.S. retail sales post smallest gain in six months
resumo: U.S. retail sales recorded their smallest gain in six months in August as consumers cut back on purchases of motor vehicles and clothing, but upward revisions to July data likely keep intact expectations of strong economic growth in the third quarter.

titulo: Wells Fargo sees total expenses dropping by $3 billion by 2020: CFO
resumo: Wells Fargo & Co's chief financial officer said on Friday the bank expects to reduce total expenses by about $3 billion by 2020.

titulo: VW board to discuss future of Audi CEO Stadler on Monday: Spiegel
resumo: The supervisory board of Volkswagen will meet on Monday to discuss the future of Rupert Stadler, the suspended chief executive of its premium brand Audi, German magazine Der Spiegel reported on Friday.

titulo: Deutsche Bank's retreat to Germany gets tepid welcome at home
resumo: German manufacturer Voith has been a customer of Deutsche Bank  for more than a century. But as the maker of hydropower and paper plants expands abroad it said it has started to hire other international banks where it would have previously considered Germany's largest lender.

titulo: Hyundai Motor Group promotes heir apparent Chung as succession looms
resumo: Hyundai Motor Group promoted heir apparent Euisun Chung on Friday to a role of overseeing the conglomerate, moving him a step closer to succeeding his octogenarian father as head of South Korea's second largest group.

titulo: Takeda gets China's approval for $62 billion Shire purchase
resumo: Takeda Pharmaceutical Co Ltd said on Friday China approved its purchase of Shire Plc, the latest regulator to clear the $62 billion deal and bring the Japanese group closer to becoming a global top 10 drugmaker.

titulo: U.S. sanctions Russian, Chinese tech firms over funds for North Korea
resumo: The United States on Thursday imposed sanctions on a China-based tech firm, its North Korean CEO and a Russian subsidiary, accusing them of moving illicit funding to North Korea in violation of U.S. sanctions.

titulo: Australia watchdog files civil suit against ANZ over 2015 share issue
resumo: Australia's corporate watchdog sued Australia and New Zealand Banking Group Ltd  on Friday over a troubled A$2.5 billion ($1.8 billion) share placement, flexing its muscle amid criticism levied at regulators for being soft on finance firms.

titulo: America Movil cuts stake in KPN to 16 percent
resumo: Mexican telecommunications company America Movil has cut its stake in Dutch telecoms company KPN to slightly more than 16 percent, a filing at financial watchdog AFM showed.

titulo: Leading Carige investors told to seek regulatory clearance over stake
resumo: Three leading investors in Italy's Carige  will have their voting rights capped at 10 percent at a key shareholder meeting next week unless they gain regulatory clearance for the their combined 15.2 percent stake, the bank said.

titulo: Xiaomi creates new management jobs aimed at CEO succession planning
resumo: Chinese smartphone maker Xiaomi Inc  said on Friday it will rejig its businesses and create new leadership positions aimed at building a second tier to CEO Lei Jun, amid increased scrutiny of governance and successions at Chinese tech firms.

titulo: Amazon's Jeff Bezos commits $2 billion to help homeless, pre-schools
resumo: Jeff Bezos, Amazon.com Inc's  founder and the world's richest person, said on Thursday he will commit $2 billion to helping homeless families and starting pre-schools for low-income communities.

titulo: China says world trade system not perfect, needs reform
resumo: The current world trade system is not perfect and China supports reforms to it, including to the World Trade Organization, to make it fairer and more effective, Beijing's top diplomat said.

titulo: FDA move on e-cigarettes means new risks for Fidelity funds
resumo: Fidelity Investments' bet on a privately held maker of e-cigarettes faces new risks after U.S. health regulators on Wednesday said they are considering a ban on flavored versions of the popular "vaping" products.

titulo: Google's China plan spurs inquiry from U.S. lawmakers, staff departures
resumo: A bipartisan group of 16 U.S. lawmakers asked Alphabet Inc's Google  on Thursday if it would comply with China's internet censorship and surveillance policies should it re-enter the Chinese search engine market.

titulo: Sears' same-store sales losses narrow but warns it may go out of business
resumo: Sears Holdings Corp  reported a smaller decline in quarterly same-store sales on Thursday, but warned again that there is doubt about the company's continued operation as it awaits approval to sell some of its businesses to its chief executive's hedge fund.

titulo: IKEA targets home delivery by electric vehicle in five major cities by 2020
resumo: IKEA Group, the owner of most IKEA stores worldwide, said on Friday all its home deliveries in the inner cities of Amsterdam, Los Angeles, New York, Paris and Shanghai would be made by electric vehicles or through other zero-emission means by 2020.

titulo: Wall Street rises with Apple, easing trade concerns
resumo: Apple led a rebound in technology shares and boosted all three major U.S. stock indexes on Thursday, while trade worries eased after China welcomed new talks with the United States.

titulo: SEC rescinds guidance that bolstered proxy advisers
resumo: The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission handed public companies a procedural victory on Thursday in their efforts to reduce the influence of proxy advisory firms, rescinding a pair of staff letters that critics said encouraged mutual funds to rely on their recommendations.

titulo: Trade hopes and Turkey rate hike feed the bulls
resumo: Signs of movement in the U.S.-China trade stand-off and an interest rate hike in emerging market trouble-spot Turkey sent an index of global stocks higher on Thursday as risk appetite returned.

titulo: UPS plan to boost business shipping falls short with investors
resumo: United Parcel Service Inc  is doubling down on shipments between businesses as part of a $20 billion overhaul aimed at automating its facilities and offsetting the profit squeeze from lower-profit home deliveries from large retailers like Amazon.com .

titulo: Canada PM says he wants a good NAFTA deal soon, hazy on deadline
resumo: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau said on Thursday he wanted a good NAFTA deal as soon as possible, but did not answer directly when asked if he agreed with Washington that the end of September was the final deadline for talks.

titulo: Atlanta Fed's Bostic: Rate hikes can continue for several more quarters
resumo: There may be room for the U.S. jobs market to improve further without pressure on inflation, an argument for the Fed to be patient as it considers further rate increases, Atlanta Federal Reserve bank president Raphael Bostic said on Thursday.

titulo: Walmart's Jet.com to offer Nike products in bid for urban consumers
resumo: Walmart's Jet.com said on Thursday it will sell Nike Inc products and offer more delivery options, beginning in its top market of New York City, in a bid to sharpen its aim for affluent, urban consumers and better compete with e-commerce rivals like Amazon.com Inc.

titulo: Company veteran named Airbus sales chief after ex-Rolls executive quits
resumo: Airbus  named its second new sales chief in less than a year on Thursday after chief commercial officer Eric Schulz abruptly resigned nine months after being poached from Rolls-Royce  to lead the battle for jet sales against Boeing.

titulo: Trump's antitrust enforcer considers shifting up a gear
resumo: The chairman of the Federal Trade Commission, which stops mergers it believes will push up prices, signaled Thursday he was willing to consider tougher enforcement, a move that could affect high profile big tech companies but also energy producers, drug makers and a big swath of the U.S. economy.

titulo: GM to recall about 1.2 million vehicles worldwide
resumo: General Motors Co  is recalling 1.205 million pickup trucks and sport utility vehicles worldwide due to issues with a temporary loss of power steering, the No.1 U.S. automaker said on Thursday.

titulo: JPMorgan sees small drop in third-quarter markets revenue vs year earlier
resumo: Third-quarter markets revenue at JPMorgan Chase & Co  is down by a small percent from a year earlier after adjusting for tax law changes, Chief Financial Officer Marianne Lake said at an investor conference on Thursday.

titulo: Second round of tariff-related aid to farmers may come in December: USDA
resumo: A second round of tariff-related aid to U.S. farmers could be announced in December, according to a white paper released by the U.S. Department of Agriculture on Thursday.

titulo: Ex-Deerfield partners get prison in case over U.S. agency leaks
resumo: Two former partners of the hedge fund Deerfield Management were sentenced to three years in prison on Thursday after being convicted of taking part in what prosecutors have described as an insider trading scheme based on leaks from within a federal healthcare agency.

titulo: Exclusive: Hedge fund Diamondback founders Schimel and Sapanski plan new fund - sources
resumo: The founders of Diamondback Capital, one of a handful of hedge funds touched by the U.S. government's insider trading probe, have reunited and are working on a new investment project, people familiar with the matter said.

titulo: VW's Skoda unions object to renewed talks on production moves
resumo: Trade unions at Volkswagen's  Czech unit Skoda Auto on Thursday rejected any idea of moving some of the production of its premium sedan model Superb to German plants.

titulo: U.S. labor board to ease companies’ liability for contractors' labor practices
resumo: The U.S. agency that enforces federal labor laws took the first steps on Thursday towards loosening an Obama-era standard that made it easier to hold companies liable for illegal labor practices by their contractors and franchisees.

titulo: Goldman names new operating chief, finance head in executive shakeup
resumo: Goldman Sachs Group Inc on Thursday announced its biggest management shakeup since naming David Solomon as Chief Executive Officer Lloyd Blankfein's successor.

titulo: Roche steps up efficiency drive to take sting out of biosimilars
resumo: Roche , the world's biggest producer of cancer drugs, is stepping up cost cuts in an efficiency drive made unavoidable by competition from cut-price copies of three mega-brands from its famed U.S. Genentech biotech stable.

titulo: Ryanair faces one-day strike in five countries over contracts
resumo: Ryanair faces a one-day walkout by cabin crew across five countries on Sept. 28 as unions step up pressure on Europe's biggest budget airline to accept local contracts.

titulo: U.S. consumer prices rise slowly; jobless claims near 49-year low
resumo: U.S. consumer prices rose less than expected in August as increases in gasoline and rents were offset by declines in healthcare and apparel costs, and underlying inflation pressures also appeared to be slowing.

titulo: Kroger misses same-store sales estimates, shares drop 11 pct
resumo: U.S. supermarket chain Kroger Co missed quarterly same-store sales estimates on Thursday, sending shares down as much as 11 percent, as changes to the way it stocks merchandise on shelves kept some customers away from its stores, analysts said.

titulo: Three Italy cabin crew unions reach labor agreement with Ryanair
resumo: Unions representing three of Ryanair's  Italy-based cabin crews said on Thursday they had reached an agreement in principle for a three-year collective labor agreement, according to a statement.

titulo: Fidelity Investments winds down $3.5 billion index fund
resumo: Fidelity Investments is winding down a fund that had more than $3 billion in assets last year, with the Boston-based money manager citing an overlap in holdings of other funds used in its target-date products to explain the rare move.

titulo: Christian Scherer appointed Airbus chief commercial officer
resumo: Airbus said in a statement it had appointed Christian Scherer as Chief Commercial Officer, replacing Eric Schulz, who has decided to leave the company for personal reasons.

titulo: U.S. consumer regulator sues pension lender for misleading borrowers
resumo: The U.S. Consumer Financial Protection Bureau has filed suit against Future Income Payments, a Nevada company that offers cash advances in exchange for future pension benefits, which the regulator said are actually exceptionally high-interest loans.

titulo: Target to hire 120,000 workers to meet holiday season rush

titulo: Global funds cut stocks to 11-month lows, eye trade war risk: Reuters poll
resumo: Global investors cut equities to 11-month lows in June and raised cash levels as the U.S. ratcheted up trade tensions, but a majority of Reuters poll participants still expect world stocks to end 2018 higher after a volatile first half.

titulo: Bitcoin skids below $6,000, hits lowest level since November
resumo: Bitcoin's value slid to its lowest level since November on Friday, as waning investor interest and recent negative headlines from global regulators weakened demand for the cryptocurrency and most of its rivals.

titulo: China's Xiaomi raises $4.72 billion after pricing HK IPO at bottom of range: sources
resumo: HONG KONG (Reuters/IFR) - China's Xiaomi Corp  priced its Hong Kong initial public offering (IPO) at the bottom of an indicative range, raising $4.72 billion in the world's biggest tech float in four years, people close to the transaction said on Friday.

titulo: China Tower wins approval for Hong Kong IPO of up to $10 billion: sources
resumo: HONG KONG (Reuters/IFR) - China Tower, the world's largest mobile mast operator, has won approval in Hong Kong for an IPO that could raise up to $10 billion, according to people familiar with the situation.

titulo: BMW signs battery order with China's CATL
resumo: BMW  has awarded a contract worth just over a billion euros ($1.16 billion) to China's lithium battery maker  Contemporary Amperex Technology Ltd (CATL) , the German carmaker said on Thursday.

titulo: Japan's Sharp ditches $2 billion share issue plan after investor backlash
resumo: Japan's Sharp Corp scrapped a plan to issue up to $2 billion in new shares, changing its mind in a matter of weeks after the initial announcement prompted investors to dump its shares on fears of earnings per share dilution.

titulo: China's penetration of Silicon Valley creates risks for startups
resumo: Danhua Capital has invested in some of Silicon Valley's most promising startups in areas like drones, artificial intelligence and cyber security. The venture capital firm is based just outside Stanford University, the epicenter of U.S. technology entrepreneurship.

titulo: Amazon, H&M and other multinationals pressing to soften Indian state's plastic ban
resumo: Multinational companies like Amazon Inc  and H&M  are lobbying to soften a ban against single-use plastic imposed by an Indian state last week, according to four sources familiar with the matter.

titulo: Legal battles, education sessions and firings: Inside the fight for unions at Samsung
resumo: When Jo Jang-hee, a restaurant manager at a Samsung amusement park, tried to set up a labor union in 2011, human resources managers gathered him and some 60 colleagues into a basement seminar room.

titulo: Trump says SoftBank Son's U.S. investment hitting $72 billion
resumo: MT. PLEASANT, Wis. - U.S. President Donald Trump said on Thursday SoftBank Group Corp  Chief Executive Masayoshi Son is increasing his investment in the United States to $72 billion, significantly more than the $50 billion he had previously pledged.

titulo: Verizon to shut down award-winning go90 mobile video app: company
resumo: Verizon Communications Inc is shutting down its mobile video service go90 less than three years after it launched, a company spokeswoman said.

titulo: Trump lauds Taiwan's Foxconn, threatens Harley on Wisconsin trip
resumo: U.S. President Donald Trump praised Taiwanese high-tech company Foxconn and vaguely threatened U.S. motorcycle maker Harley-Davidson on a campaign-style trip on Thursday to Wisconsin, a state that supported him in the 2016 election.

titulo: China's ZTE still in limbo over U.S. Commerce ban

titulo: BHP pays out $211 million towards Samarco dam accident rehabilitation

titulo: Brazil's Vale to pay 557.7 million reais for Samarco damage in 2018

titulo: U.S. manufacturers, steel makers battle over tariff relief

titulo: U.S. gas pipeline industry wants help beating back green groups

titulo: California lawmakers approve data-privacy bill opposed by Silicon Valley
resumo: California Governor Jerry Brown on Thursday signed data privacy legislation aimed at giving consumers more control over how companies collect and manage their personal information, a proposal that Google and other big companies had opposed as too burdensome.

titulo: Ex-Morgan Stanley adviser sentenced to U.S. prison for fraud

resumo: Amazon.com Inc said on Thursday it would buy small online pharmacy PillPack, a move that will put the world's biggest online retailer in direct competition with drugstore chains, drug distributors and pharmacy benefit managers.

titulo: Fox shareholders to vote on Disney's offer on July 27

titulo: Starbucks CFO Maw to retire, shares tumble

titulo: Wall Street advances with help from technology, financials

titulo: Russian tycoon Magomedov steps down from Virgin Hyperloop One board

titulo: Bitter pill for U.S. drug supply chains as Amazon enters the scene
resumo: ** Pharma supply chain hit by Amazon's move to buy online pharmacy PillPack, which offers pre-sorted medicine doses

titulo: UK takeover panel to review impact of Disney bid for Fox on Sky

titulo: Visa, Mastercard close to settling issues over card-swipe fees: WSJ

resumo: Amazon.com Inc  on Wednesday said it would offer incentives to entice entrepreneurs to set up their own small package-delivery  businesses as part of Amazon's latest effort to solve the challenge of getting goods the last mile to customer doorsteps.

titulo: Factbox: Crossfire of tariffs as Trump rattles world trade order
resumo: U.S. President Donald Trump has rattled the world trade order this year by seeking to renegotiate the terms of some of the United States' trading relationships, in particular with China. He has imposed tariffs on some imports, in turn sparking retaliatory action by other countries, raising fears of a global trade war.

titulo: Exclusive: China shuns U.S. request for talks on airline website dispute over Taiwan
resumo: China has rejected U.S. requests for talks over how U.S. airlines and their websites refer to Chinese-claimed Taiwan, according to sources, including a U.S. official, adding to tensions in a relationship already frayed by a major trade dispute.

titulo: Takeda shareholders bin proposal from anti-Shire group; only 10 percent in favor

titulo: UK accounts watchdog probes Deloitte's audit of SIG's 2015, 2016 statements
resumo: UK's accounting watchdog said on Thursday it began an investigation into Deloitte LLP's audit of two annual financial statements of SIG Plc  and is focusing on the audit of cash and supplier rebates.

titulo: Walmart rolls out 3D virtual online shopping
resumo: Walmart Inc said late Wednesday it is introducing a 3D virtual shopping tour on its website that would help customers browse and choose items for their homes.

resumo: The rapidly deteriorating trade and investment relationship between Washington and Beijing is sending a further chill through Chinese dealmakers who have already seen the number of Chinese acquisitions of American assets take a big hit.

resumo: Walt Disney Co won U.S. approval to buy Twenty-First Century Fox Inc entertainment assets for $71.3 billion on condition it sell Fox's 22 regional sports networks, the Justice Department said on Wednesday, giving Disney an edge over Comcast Corp's competing bid.

titulo: Chipotle CEO plans marketing blitz, closing up to 65 stores
resumo: Chipotle Mexican Grill Inc  said on Wednesday the chain will shut up to 65 underperforming restaurants and revamp its marketing under Chief Executive Officer Brian Niccol, but many questions remain unanswered about what to expect under the new leadership.

titulo: UK watchdog may expand probe to include insider trading at collapsed Carillion
resumo: Britain's markets watchdog may expand an investigation into the failed outsourcing firm Carillion to examine whether there was illegal trading of shares before the company's collapse.

titulo: After bankruptcy, Gibson plots return to basics, and possibly ukuleles
resumo: As Gibson Brands Inc targets an exit from Chapter 11 bankruptcy later this year, the iconic guitar maker plans a return to traditional guitars after an ill-fated attempt at computerizing them and possibly a foray into ukuleles, CEO Henry Juszkiewicz told Reuters on Wednesday.

titulo: Apple, Samsung settle U.S. patent dispute

titulo: Wall Street tumbles on renewed U.S.-China trade jitters
resumo: U.S. stocks fell on Wednesday on renewed uncertainty regarding the U.S. stance on Chinese investments in American technology companies, reversing gains earlier in the session.

titulo: Exclusive: Bahrain Steel says Anglo declared force majeure on contract after spills

titulo: Stocks sputter globally as investors shed optimism, oil caps losses

titulo: Canada cannot start work on Trans Mountain pipeline until deal closes: minister

titulo: Boeing's engine proposal deadline looms for new mid-market jet: report
resumo: Three of the largest engine makers face a Wednesday deadline to provide Boeing Co  with formal proposals for how they would power the planemaker's possible new mid-market jet, an aviation magazine reported.

titulo: Conagra to buy Pinnacle for $8.1 billion, creating frozen food powerhouse

titulo: U.S. business spending on equipment cooling; trade gap narrowing
resumo: New orders for U.S.-made capital goods and shipments unexpectedly fell in May, but upward revisions to data for the prior month pointed to moderate growth in business spending on equipment in the second quarter.

titulo: Market rate rise may thwart Fed's balance sheet plan
resumo: The rise of a key U.S. overnight interest rate has raised speculation that higher bank borrowing costs will force the Federal Reserve to stop shrinking its balance sheet sooner than planned.

titulo: Manulife plans to free up $3.8 billion in capital by 2022
resumo: Manulife , Canada's biggest insurer, said on Wednesday it had set a target to free up C$5 billion ($3.8 billion) in capital over the next three years, partly by offloading businesses that it no longer wants.

titulo: Largest banks clear U.S. Fed's toughest annual stress tests

titulo: Mnuchin says new investment controls needed to protect U.S. tech: CNBC
resumo: U.S. Treasury Secretary Steve Mnuchin defended the Trump administration's decision to use legislation to strengthen investment controls, saying certain countries will receive heightened review under changes aimed at protecting U.S. technology.

resumo: Fujifilm Holdings Corp  on Wednesday threatened to compete against Xerox Corp  in Asia-Pacific and challenge it in America and Europe if it failed to renew its technology agreement in 2021.

titulo: Surprises lurk in Trump's China tariff list, from thermostats to vaping devices

titulo: Google retires DoubleClick, AdWords brand names
resumo: Alphabet Inc's  Google announced on Wednesday the biggest-ever rebranding of its advertising software, retiring the DoubleClick and AdWords brands to streamline entry points for advertisers and ad sellers.

titulo: Short on workers, German companies offer more employee flexibility
resumo: German companies are so desperate to attract staff that they are falling over themselves to offer perks such as long holidays, shorter hours, flexible shifts and sabbaticals, even though employees here already work the fewest hours in the developed world.

titulo: Exclusive: Ackman makes fresh layoffs at Pershing Square hedge fund
resumo: Billionaire hedge fund manager William Ackman, whose investment assets have shrunk by more than half in the last three years, has made a second round of staff cuts and laid off three investor relations team members, two sources familiar with the matter said on Tuesday.

titulo: Chinese telco Huawei lobbies hard to allay Australia's security concerns
resumo: Chinese telecom firm Huawei Technologies Co Ltd's [HWL.UL] network equipment is "safe and secure", its Australian chairman said on Wednesday, ramping up the company's public lobbying against concerns that its links to China pose a security risk.

titulo: Ex-Akebia employee jailed for night as insider trading trial starts

titulo: Battery in fatal Florida Tesla crash reignited twice: NTSB report
resumo: A Tesla Inc Model S involved in a May 8 fatal high-speed crash in Florida reignited twice after firefighters extinguished a fire in the electric vehicle, the National Transportation Safety Board (NTSB) said in a preliminary report on Tuesday.

titulo: U.S. seeks to export its 'energy miracle' even as trade tensions mount
resumo: The administration of President Donald Trump wants to help allies replicate the U.S. natural gas and oil production boom by exporting American know-how, U.S. Energy Secretary Rick Perry said on Tuesday, even as trade disputes intensified.

titulo: Chevron, Exxon CEOs worry global trade conflict could harm economy
resumo: The leaders of two of the world's largest energy companies said on Tuesday they worry a trade conflict between the United States and other nations could destabilize the global economy.

titulo: British politicians step up pressure on Lloyds Bank over HBOS fraud
resumo: Lloyds Banking Group  is facing more pressure over its handling of a fraud at its HBOS Reading business that took place more than a decade ago, with the chair of Britain's Treasury Committee calling for "maximum transparency" over the affair.
