titulo: GE's determined makeover could dim deal prospects
resumo: General Electric Co's plan to divest $20 billion worth of assets may have bankers excited about contacting potential buyers, breaking off other big chunks of the conglomerate, and creating an M&A bonanza.

titulo: Trump says security panel can protect U.S. technology from China
resumo: President Donald Trump on Tuesday endorsed U.S. Treasury Secretary Steven Mnuchin's measured approach to restricting Chinese investments in U.S. technology companies, saying a strengthened merger security review committee could protect sensitive American technologies.

titulo: Put on probation, Uber wins London license to avoid ban
resumo: Uber Technologies Inc won a probationary license to operate in London on Tuesday in a partial victory for its new chief executive after it made changes to ease strained relations with city authorities.

titulo: The culture clash behind GE's quick exit from Baker Hughes stake
resumo: When General Electric Co bought oilfield services giant Baker Hughes last July, it created a global industry colossus with $22 billion in annual revenue.

titulo: Energy leads Wall Street rebound as trade worries ease
resumo: U.S. stocks found their footing on Tuesday, helped by gains in the energy, technology and consumer discretionary sectors after a sharp sell-off a day earlier on spiraling global trade tensions.

titulo: U.S. senators want Trump to reconsider lifting ban on China's ZTE
resumo: Two U.S. senators urged President Donald Trump on Tuesday to reconsider his agreement with ZTE Corp , saying lifting a ban on China's second-largest telecommunications maker  poses "a significant threat" to national security.

titulo: Global stocks, oil rise on U.S. pressure to snub Iranian crude
resumo: Rising shares of energy and technology companies helped global stock markets regain ground on Tuesday, a day after a mounting trade fight pummeled equities, while oil surged more than 2 percent as Washington lobbied against Iranian crude imports.

titulo: Oil surges as U.S. pushes allies to halt imports of Iranian crude

titulo: Dollar rebounds as trade worries hurt high-yielding currencies
resumo: The dollar rose across the board on Tuesday, as lingering global trade tensions prompted traders to ditch most high-yielding currencies and investors focused on expectations the Federal Reserve will continue to raise interest rates.

titulo: U.S. charges S&P analyst, 2 others with insider trading in paint merger
resumo: The U.S. government on Tuesday criminally charged a Standard & Poor's credit ratings analyst and two friends, all from Manhattan, with insider trading related to Sherwin-Williams Co's $9.3 billion purchase of Valspar Corp.

titulo: Ex-State Street executive convicted in U.S. of hidden fee fraud

titulo: Facebook eases ban on cryptocurrency related ads

titulo: China says it does not want U.S. trade frictions to escalate

titulo: Macau clamps down on use of UnionPay cards to dodge currency controls
resumo: Authorities in Macau, the world's biggest gambling hub, have told financial institutions to tackle the illegal use of UnionPay cards to evade exchange controls, China's latest move to clamp down on illicit capital outflows.

titulo: Icahn buys small stake in drugmaker Allergan: sources
resumo: Billionaire investor Carl Icahn has acquired a small stake in Allergan Plc  at a time when the drugmaker is under pressure from other activist shareholders, people familiar with the matter said on Wednesday.

titulo: U.S. lawmakers press Facebook over Chinese data sharing
resumo: Facebook Inc faced criticism on Wednesday from Republican and Democratic U.S. lawmakers who demanded that the social media company be more forthcoming about data it has shared with four Chinese firms.

titulo: Congress has few options to stop Trump from saving China's ZTE
resumo: There is little Congress can do to stop President Donald Trump if he wants to relax penalties on ZTE Corp , despite strong bipartisan opposition to easing restrictions on China's No. 2 telecommunications equipment maker.

titulo: Wall Street rises on economic data, easing trade worries
resumo: Wall Street indexes rallied on Wednesday with help from financial stocks as investors eyed strong economic data and trade war fears took a back seat while the Nasdaq registered its third straight record closing high.

titulo: Trump's move to please farmers on biofuels reform draws refinery union ire
resumo: U.S. President Donald Trump, in yielding to pressure from farming states and agreeing to suspend changes to U.S. biofuel policy is now being criticized by another important constituency, the main union for oil refinery workers.

titulo: Euro rises to near two-week high, yields jump after ECB comments
resumo: The euro rose to a nearly two-week high while the 10-year U.S. Treasury yield hit a 1-1/2-week peak on Wednesday after officials said the European Central Bank could wind down its stimulus program by the end of the year.

titulo: Wal-Mart settles discrimination lawsuit by transgender worker
resumo: Wal-Mart Stores Inc  on Wednesday settled a lawsuit by a transgender former employee in North Carolina who accused the retail giant of unlawfully firing her for complaining about harassment.

titulo: Mexico's Walmex says same-store sales up 7.2 percent in May
resumo: Mexico's biggest retailer, Wal-Mart de Mexico , said on Wednesday that same-store sales in Mexico rose in May by 7.2 percent.

titulo: Paul Dacre, editor of the UK's Daily Mail, to stand down
resumo: The editor of Britain's Daily Mail tabloid Paul Dacre will stand down after 26 years in November to become chairman and editor-in-chief of the title's publisher, Associated Newspapers, Daily Mail and General Trust  said on Wednesday.

titulo: Drugmaker Lundbeck to settle U.S. charity probe for $52.6 million
resumo: Danish drugmaker Lundbeck  said on Wednesday that it will pay $52.6 million to resolve a U.S. probe into its financial support of patient assistance charitable foundations.

titulo: CP Rail deal will give workers 9 percent raise over four years: union
resumo: Canadian Pacific Railway Ltd conductors and locomotive engineers will get a 9 percent salary hike over four years as part of a tentative agreement reached last week with the Teamsters, a union spokesman said on Wednesday.

titulo: Facebook confirms data sharing with Chinese companies
resumo: Facebook Inc  said Tuesday it has data sharing partnerships with at least four Chinese companies including Huawei, the world's third largest smartphone maker, which has come under scrutiny from U.S. intelligence agencies on security concerns.

titulo: Oil falls on U.S. crude build; global supply seen rising
resumo: Oil prices fell on Wednesday on worries that global supply is climbing after U.S. inventories rose unexpectedly and Saudi Arabia and other big producers signaled that they may increase output.

titulo: U.S. dollar-store shares marked down but specialty discounters thrive
resumo: The shares of so-called dollar stores have suffered as U.S. shoppers have started spending more at bargain retailers with focused concepts and big discount chains.

titulo: Tesla shares rise as Musk says Model 3 set to hit target
resumo: Tesla Inc  shares headed for their best day in more than two years on Wednesday after Chief Executive Elon Musk said the car maker was now churning out 500 of its Model 3 sedans a day and should achieve its 5,000 per week target by the end of June.

titulo: Brown-Forman upped Jack Daniel's shipments as trade war loomed
resumo: Jack Daniel's maker Brown-Forman Corp   warned on Wednesday of trouble ahead from tariffs being slapped on U.S. bourbon and said it had been working in recent months to mitigate the potential impact by shipping more liquor overseas.

titulo: Alphabet shareholders reject diversity proposal backed by employees
resumo: Alphabet Inc's shareholders, including top executives, voted down several proposals on Wednesday, defeating campaigns to tie pay to diversity goals and to get the Google parent to provide more data about efforts to moderate user-generated content.

titulo: UBS cuts at least 100 jobs in asset management arm: Bloomberg
resumo: Swiss bank UBS Group AG  is cutting jobs at its asset management unit while it focuses on growth in China along with passive and sustainable investing, Bloomberg said on Wednesday.

titulo: Oil vs wild rice: Enbridge, opponents gear up final U.S. pipeline push
resumo: Canada's Enbridge Inc  faces an indefinite delay to its biggest-ever capital project if a U.S. state regulator denies its preferred pipeline route through Minnesota, but a victory would spell relief for Canadian oil shippers and northern U.S. refiners.

titulo: VW sees production stoppages due to new testing rules
resumo: Volkswagen  faces production stoppages next quarter due to bottlenecks caused by new lab tests for emissions and fuel consumption, and will discuss the implications for workers in the coming days, it said on Wednesday.

titulo: Delta Air shares slip as rising fuel costs weigh on profits
resumo: Delta Air Lines  on Wednesday lowered its second quarter profit forecast due to rising fuel costs, and its shares fell as much as nearly 4 percent before trimming those losses.

titulo: Uber jumps into Europe's crowded bike-sharing market
resumo: Uber, the ride-hailing company which has been banned from many European cities, is about to launch its JUMP electric bicycle sharing service in Germany as it diversifies and seeks to build bridges with local authorities.

titulo: Germany's E.ON puts fate of Uniper management in Fortum's hands
resumo: A vote that could have triggered a probe into whether managers of German energy company Uniper  tried to block the sale of a 47 percent stake to Finland's Fortum  was postponed on Wednesday in line with a proposal by Uniper's biggest shareholder E.ON .

titulo: Rising exports push U.S. trade deficit to seven-month low
resumo: The U.S. trade deficit fell to a seven-month low in April as exports rose to a record high, lifted by an increase in shipments of industrial materials and soybeans.

titulo: Property investors in Austria give Starwood the cold shoulder
resumo: U.S. investment firm Starwood Capital failed on Wednesday in its attempt to take major stakes in Austrian property groups CA Immo  and Immofinanz , as shareholders bet a more lucrative deal could be secured.

titulo: Athenahealth CEO steps down; company considers sale
resumo: Athenahealth Inc's  Chief Executive Officer Jonathan Bush stepped down on Wednesday and the healthcare software maker said it was exploring options, including selling itself, following pressure from activist investor Elliott Management.

titulo: PetSmart taps advisers to trim $8 billion debt pile: sources
resumo: PetSmart Inc, the largest U.S. pet retailer, has hired restructuring advisers to explore ways to trim its debt pile of more than $8 billion as it continues to face falling profits, according to people familiar with the matter.

titulo: Qatar Airways boss apologizes for remarks on women CEOs
resumo: The head of Qatar Airways apologized on Wednesday for saying that a woman could not do his job, while global airlines pledged to speed up efforts to break down gender imbalances in aviation.

titulo: Indian police source says still seeking to quiz AirAsia boss; airline denies summons
resumo: India's federal police will again ask AirAsia Group's  chief to appear for questioning over allegations the airline broke rules to obtain a flying license in the country, a source said on Wednesday, after he failed to answer a previous request.

titulo: Foxconn looks for its next act but faces risks as it diversifies
resumo: Foxconn, the Taiwanese contract manufacturer that rose to global prominence as the manufacturer of the Apple iPhone, is trying to reinvent itself as smartphone sales plateau and Apple Inc diversifies its supplier base.

titulo: Swiss prosecutors will not pursue Novartis over Trump lawyer payments
resumo: The Swiss attorney general's office (OAG) said on Wednesday it will not bring proceedings against Novartis in connection with $1.2 million in payments the Swiss drugmaker made to U.S. President Donald Trump's personal lawyer.

titulo: Pfizer pumps $600 million into venture capital arm
resumo: Pfizer Inc  said on Wednesday it planned to invest $600 million in its venture capital fund, a quarter of which would be earmarked for promising research on neuroscience.

titulo: Airlines warned against complacency after dream safety run
resumo: Global airlines, coming off a record-low accident rate in 2017, need to guard against complacency over safety as heavy growth in travel demand stretches the air transport system, industry leaders warned at a conference this week.

titulo: U.S. house prices to rise at twice the speed of inflation and pay: Reuters poll
resumo: An acute shortage of affordable homes in the United States will continue over the coming year, according to a majority of property market analysts polled by Reuters, driving prices up faster than inflation and wage growth.

titulo: Airlines struggle with global pilot shortage
resumo: A growing shortage of airline pilots is putting the industry's recent growth at risk as planes sit idle, higher salaries cut into profits and unions across the globe push for more benefits.

titulo: G7 likely to discuss global economy, concerns about U.S. trade policy: Japan's Suga
resumo: Japan's top government spokesman said on Wednesday that the Group of Seven leaders were likely to discuss the global economy and concerns about U.S. trade policy.

titulo: Japan PM Abe: no countries benefit from trade protectionism
resumo: Japanese Prime Minister Shinzo Abe said on Wednesday that he wants to send a message at a Group of Seven leaders' summit that no countries benefit from trade protectionism.

titulo: Fresenius transfers inpatient rehabilitation business to Vamed unit
resumo: German healthcare group Fresenius  said it was transferring its inpatient rehabilitation business from its private hospital operations to its Fresenius Vamed unit to better enable continued growth at the two businesses.

titulo: Siemens signs pacts to deepen ties to China's Belt and Road push
resumo: Siemens  has signed more than ten cooperation agreements with Chinese companies as it seeks to benefit from deepening involvement with China's "Belt and Road" infrastructure initiative, the German engineering group said on Wednesday.

titulo: World's top wealth fund opposed Musk's double role at Tesla
resumo: Norway's $1 trillion wealth fund said on Wednesday it had backed an initiative to wrest the role of chairman away from Tesla  Chief Executive Elon Musk.

titulo: Air Baltic's hurry for Bombardier CSeries deal foreshadows jet selling spree
resumo: Latvia-based AirBaltic hastened a deal for Bombardier  CSeries planes before Airbus formally takes over the Canadian planemaker because it was worried about securing the right delivery slots, the carrier's chief executive said on Wednesday.

titulo: Alibaba's Cainiao JV to invest $1.53 billion in Hong Kong logistics hub
resumo: Alibaba Group Holding Ltd's  logistics affiliate said on Wednesday that it would lead a joint venture to invest $1.53 billion in a new logistics center at Hong Kong's airport as part of its efforts to expand its global delivery network.

titulo: Exclusive: China's ZTE signed preliminary agreement to lift U.S. ban: sources
resumo: ZTE Corp  has signed an agreement in principle that would lift a U.S. Commerce Department ban on buying from U.S. suppliers, allowing China's No. 2 telecommunications equipment maker to get back into business, according to sources familiar with the matter.

titulo: Online shopping boom keeps hopes high for air freight profits
resumo: An explosion of e-commerce demand is buoying the air freight business, boosting profit at some major airlines despite global trade tensions and underscoring robust consumer confidence.

titulo: Honda to start selling Hondajet in Japan next year
resumo: Honda Motor Co  on Wednesday said it would start selling its six-seater business jet in Japan next year, as it seeks to expand global sales by tapping into demand from a growing class of high net-worth individuals.

titulo: Volatility may hit Wall Street as Alphabet, Facebook leave tech sector group
resumo: Volatility could well be in the cards for Wall Street again early this fall, but not for the same reason stocks got rattled in February.

titulo: Mexico minister sees more than 50 percent chance of initial NAFTA deal this year
resumo: Mexico's economy minister said on Tuesday that he sees a better than 50 percent chance of reaching an agreement in principle on NAFTA this year despite escalating tensions between his country and the United States.

titulo: French group Thales targets more sales and profit growth for 2018-2021
resumo: French defense electronics group Thales  set out targets for further sales and profit growth over the 2018-2021 period, and added it would increase spending on research as part of its plans to boost earnings and improve its business.

titulo: Tesla 'quite likely' to meet Model 3 goal; Musk remains chairman
resumo: Tesla Inc Chief Executive Elon Musk said on Tuesday that building 5,000 Model 3s per week by the end of June was "quite likely" as the company's production lines were now demonstrating the ability to make 3,500 vehicles per week.

titulo: Morocco's Centrale Danone workers protest job losses due to boycott
resumo: Workers at Morocco's Centrale Danone, a subsidiary of French group Danone , staged a sit-in in Rabat late on Tuesday over losing their jobs as a consumer boycott now in its second month pressures the company.

titulo: Outside investors rebuke Facebook vote structure, tallies show
resumo: A wide majority of outside investors at Facebook Inc backed a measure that would revamp its voting structure and end the supervoting rights of shares held by Mark Zuckerberg and others, seen as a rebuke to the chief executive of the world's largest social media company.

titulo: Microsoft expands program for women returning to work
resumo: Microsoft Corp  is expanding a program designed to increase its hiring of women seeking to rejoin the workforce, following a rash of complaints about sexual harassment and gender discrimination at the world's largest software company.

titulo: Northrop Grumman wins U.S. antitrust approval to buy Orbital ATK
resumo: U.S. defense contractor Northrop Grumman  won U.S. antitrust approval to buy solid rocket motor supplier Orbital ATK Inc  with conditions, the Federal Trade Commission said on Tuesday.

titulo: Tronc's top shareholder terminates sale of stake to McCormick Media
resumo: Newspaper publisher Tronc Inc's  top shareholder said in a filing http://services.corporate-ir.net/SEC.Enhanced/SecCapsule.aspx?c=254385&fid=15681381 on Wednesday that it had terminated the sale of a stake in the company after the prospective buyer, investor group McCormick Media, breached its obligations.

titulo: Advent plans conversion, not expansion of Walmart's Brazil ops
resumo: Advent International, the buyout firm acquiring 80 percent of Walmart Inc's  Brazilian operations, plans to invest in converting store formats instead of opening new ones, an executive said on Tuesday.

titulo: U.S. retailers, restaurants say tax law 'drafting error' delays investments
resumo: A coalition of retailers and restaurants on Tuesday asked the U.S. Congress to fix a "drafting error" in December's tax-cut bill that they said is deterring companies from investing in their stores.

titulo: Nasdaq hits record closing high again; euro recovers
resumo: The Nasdaq hit a record closing high for the second day in a row on Tuesday, helped by tech and consumer discretionary shares, while the euro recovered after Italy's new prime minister said the government had never considered leaving the euro zone.

titulo: HP sees up to 5,000 job cuts as part of restructuring plan
resumo: HP Inc  now expects 4,500 to 5,000 employees to leave the company by the end of fiscal 2019 as part of an ongoing restructuring plan, the PC maker said on Tuesday.

titulo: Britain clears way for Murdoch to battle Comcast for Sky
resumo: Rupert Murdoch faces a 22 billion pound ($29.3 billion) fight with U.S. cable company Comcast for European pay-TV company Sky after Britain cleared his bid providing he sells off its TV news business.

titulo: Brent gains after touching one-month low on supply concerns
resumo: Brent crude reversed losses on Tuesday, after hitting its lowest price in nearly a month following a report the U.S. government asked Saudi Arabia and other major exporters to increase oil output.

titulo: Nasdaq ends at record close, S&P edges higher on data
resumo: The Nasdaq closed at a record high for the second day in a row with help from the technology and consumer discretionary sectors, while the S&P 500 edged higher as investors eyed solid U.S. economic data.

titulo: BlackRock promises selective fee cuts to capture growth
resumo: BlackRock Inc , the world's largest asset manager, on Tuesday said it would continue to reduce fees on a small group of its products with the highest growth potential, days after cutting fees on 11 exchange-traded funds.

titulo: America Movil to launch new U.S. unit for business clients
resumo: Mexican telecommunications company America Movil, controlled by the family of Mexican billionaire Carlos Slim, is launching a U.S.-based unit that will offer information technology and communications to business customers, according to a source and a document on the company's website.

titulo: Expect disagreements over tariffs at G7 summit: Canada official
resumo: The Group of Seven leading industrial nations at its summit this week is poised to disagree over U.S. metals tariffs, which have infuriated Canada and European Union members of the bloc, a Canadian official said on Tuesday.

titulo: Starbucks investors mourn end of an era as Schultz exits
resumo: Starbucks Corp's  shares fell as much as 3 percent on Tuesday as the departure of Howard Schultz, the entrepreneur who turned 11 cafes into a global juggernaut, added to a series of worrying headlines for the world's biggest coffee chain.

titulo: UBS hires Solon Kentas to lead U.S. consumer and retail M&A
resumo: UBS Group AG  has appointed Solon Kentas, previously a managing director at Deutsche Bank AG , to lead the Swiss bank's U.S. consumer and retail dealmaking efforts, UBS announced internally in a memo on Tuesday.

titulo: Wells Fargo pulls back from U.S. Midwest, selling 52 branches to Flagstar
resumo: Wells Fargo & Co  is pulling back from retail banking in the U.S. Midwest, selling all of its branches in three states, as the bank embarks on a broader review of branch profitability across the country.

titulo: G7 to pledge joint defense of democracies from foreign threats: EU official
resumo: G7 leaders will commit at their summit this week to cooperate in defending democracies from foreign threats and establish a response mechanism for that purpose, a senior EU official said on Tuesday.

titulo: G7 to uphold Russia sanctions: senior EU official
resumo: G7 leaders are likely to uphold sanctions against Russia over Moscow's annexation of Crimea and its activity in eastern Ukraine because the conditions to lift them, spelled out in the Minsk agreement, have not been met, a senior EU official said.

titulo: G7 to call on North Korea to get rid of nuclear weapons: senior EU official
resumo: G7 leaders will call on North Korea on Friday to get rid of its nuclear weapons if it wants sanctions against the country to be lifted, a senior European union official said on Tuesday.

titulo: Two Allergan shareholders call for split of chairman-CEO role
resumo: Two of Allergan Plc's  shareholders, hedge funds Appaloosa Management and Senator Investment Group, asked the drugmaker's board to split the role of chief executive officer and chairman as well as reconsider its acquisition strategy.

titulo: U.S. companies continued stock buyback frenzy in May: report
resumo: U.S. companies continued a shopping spree for their own shares in May, making $173.6 billion in buyback announcements, the highest monthly total ever, according to a research report.

titulo: David Koch to retire from Koch Industries due to poor health: memo
resumo: Conservative billionaire industrialist David Koch is retiring from his roles at Koch Industries and associated companies due to poor health, according to an internal memo seen by Reuters.

titulo: Want to extend the bull market by $6 trillion? Add women
resumo: The secret to unlocking a further $6 trillion of gains in global stocks and adding another ten years to this bull run? Forget tax cuts and share buybacks, women are key, according to research by S&P Global.

titulo: Saudi Aramco names new head of finance after government reshuffle: sources
resumo: Saudi Aramco has named six new heads of departments including its finance unit after a government reshuffle last week saw several executives at the oil giant moving to other state posts, the company and industry sources said on Tuesday.

titulo: Trump may seek separate trade deals with Canada, Mexico: U.S. adviser
resumo: U.S. President Donald Trump may seek separate talks with Canada and Mexico in a bid to get individual trade deals with the two countries, White House economic adviser Larry Kudlow said on Tuesday.

titulo: Airlines hope flashier content for travel agents boosts sales
resumo: Airlines are betting that a new system for showcasing their wares on travel agents' screens will help sell fancier seats, tastier meals, lounge access and flight options - and give profits a lift.

titulo: Twitter shares jump 5 percent as they win new follower: The S&P 500
resumo: Consider it the ultimate "like" for a stock: Twitter Inc is joining the benchmark S&P 500 U.S. index, pushing the social media company's stock price up 5 percent on Tuesday as demand surged from equities investors.

titulo: U.S. services sector activity picks up; job openings at record high
resumo: U.S. services sector activity accelerated in May, pointing to robust economic growth in the second quarter, but trade tariffs and a shortage of workers posed a threat to the outlook.

titulo: Ex-State Street executive cheated clients with secret fees: U.S. prosecutor
resumo: A former State Street Corp  executive directed a fraudulent scheme to overcharge several of the bank's clients, including Kuwait's sovereign wealth fund, by applying secret commissions to billions of dollars' worth of trades, a U.S. prosecutor told jurors on Tuesday.

titulo: StanChart plans to bring Asian operations under two main hubs
resumo: Standard Chartered  is drawing up plans to consolidate its Asian operations into two hubs - one in Hong Kong and one in Singapore - in order to better manage its capital, a source familiar with the matter said on Tuesday.

titulo: Short sellers and options traders bet on more Deutsche Bank pain
resumo: Short sellers and derivatives traders are betting Deutsche Bank's  share price recovery will prove temporary, with data suggesting short interest in the German lender is the highest of any global bank.

titulo: Russian regulator says 19,696 Peugeot and Citroen cars recalled
resumo: Russia's standards agency said on Tuesday it had been informed about the voluntary recall of 19,696 Peugeot and Citroen cars sold between February 2008 and September 2014.

titulo: A decade after debut, first A380 jumbos to be broken up
resumo: A German investment company said on Tuesday it would strip two unwanted Airbus A380 superjumbo passenger jets for parts after failing to find an airline willing to keep them flying following a decision by Singapore Airlines not to keep them in service.

titulo: China launches DRAM chip price probe into Samsung Elec, SK Hynix and Micron
resumo: China has launched a probe into Samsung Electronics Co Ltd , SK Hynix Inc  and Micron Technology Inc , the three semiconductor makers who control the market for DRAM memory chips.

titulo: Britain takes $2.68 billion loss on RBS share sale
resumo: Britain has sold some of its holding in Royal Bank of Scotland , the bank which it rescued in the 2008 financial crisis, but has taken a loss of more than 2 billion pounds ($2.68 billion) on the deal.

titulo: Airline boss stirs gender debate with provocative comment
resumo: Promises by the global airline industry to do more to promote gender equality veered off course when one of its top executives suggested his CEO role was too difficult for a woman.

titulo: Australia charges former local heads of Citi, Deutsche, ANZ in cartel case
resumo: Australian authorities have charged the former local bosses of Citigroup Inc and Deutsche Bank AG with "criminal cartel offences" over a $2.3 billion stock issue, one of the country's biggest cases of alleged white-collar crime.

titulo: Voestalpine CEO to step down in 2019
resumo: Voestalpine  Chief Executive Wolfgang Eder will step down in the summer of 2019 after some 15 years at the helm and move to the supervisory board, the Austrian speciality steel company said on Tuesday.

titulo: Sharp to buy Toshiba PC business, issue $1.8 billion in new shares
resumo: Japan's Sharp Corp  said it will buy Toshiba Corp's  personal computer business and issue $1.8 billion in new shares to buy back preferred stock from banks, highlighting a swift recovery under the control of Foxconn.

titulo: Japan's Sharp to issue new shares worth about $1.8 billion
resumo: Japan's Sharp Corp  said on Tuesday it will issue new shares worth about 200 billion yen ($1.8 billion), sending shares of the company tumbling.

titulo: Air traffic strikes bigger threat than fuel prices in 2018, IAG says
resumo: Air traffic control strikes are more of a threat to European airlines this year than a rise in fuel prices, the chief executive of British Airways-owner IAG  said on Tuesday.

titulo: Australia charges investment banks with alleged cartel activity
resumo: Australia's anti-cartel laws have come to global attention with the country's competition watchdog lodging criminal charges for alleged cartel behavior against two global investment banks, in a move one expert called "almost unique".

titulo: Japan regulators raid Kobe Steel over data tampering
resumo: Kobe Steel Ltd  said on Tuesday it had been raided by Japanese regulators over a data tampering scandal revealed last year which had continued for almost 50 years.

titulo: Europe up against Asian juggernaut in electric car battery drive
resumo: Chinese electric vehicle giant BYD  is looking at launching battery production in Europe, joining Asian rivals aiming to cash in on a green car revolution and threatening attempts by Brussels to nurture a home-grown industry.

titulo: Factbox: Plans for electric car battery production in Europe
resumo: China's BYD   is looking at launching battery production in Europe, the latest Asian battery maker aiming to make inroads in Europe as Brussels seek to build a home-grown industry to fuel a surge in electric car production.

titulo: Toyota's May China vehicle sales +8.6 Percent year on year
resumo: Toyota Motor Corp's  vehicle sales in China rose 8.6 percent in May from a year earlier to 122,400 vehicles, the company said on Tuesday.

titulo: Mexico firms eye workaround on U.S. metals tariffs; consumers in bind
resumo: Mexican firms will need to cut deals with suppliers and consider buying goods elsewhere once a conflict over U.S. steel and aluminum import tariffs starts to bite, even as consumers are seen ultimately picking up the tab from any higher prices.

titulo: Qatar Airways could face 2nd straight annual loss amid Gulf crisis: CEO
resumo: Qatar Airways could face a second straight year of losses in 2018 amid a regional dispute that has banned the airline from four Arab countries, the airline's CEO said on Tuesday.

titulo: GM China raises new-energy vehicle target to 20 models through 2023
resumo: General Motors Co  is accelerating the pace of electric vehicle (EV) launches in China as policymakers in the world's biggest auto market continue to promote EVs as an alternative to gasoline cars.

titulo: Emirates sees oil, dollar 'double whammy', but demand strong
resumo: The president of Emirates airline said the rare combination of higher oil and a stronger dollar, which usually move in opposite directions, represented a "double whammy" in terms of their impact on costs but that passenger demand remained strong.

titulo: Chinese airlines apply fuel surcharge after three-year hiatus
resumo: Chinese carriers including Air China   and China Eastern Airlines Corp   will apply a fuel surcharge to domestic routes from Tuesday, the first time they have done so in three years as oil prices begin to rise.

titulo: Japan's Sharp says to buy Toshiba's PC business
resumo: Japan's Sharp Corp said it will acquire Toshiba Corp's personal computer business for $36 million, highlighting its recovery under the control of Foxconn and marking a return to a business it quit eight years ago.

titulo: Exclusive: Mexico set to impose 20 percent tariff on U.S. pork legs - sources
resumo: Mexico will impose a 20 percent tariff on U.S. pork imports, two industry officials with direct knowledge of the plan told Reuters on Monday, for the first time providing details of the country's retaliatory measures to U.S. President Donald Trump's tariffs on steel and aluminum.

titulo: Starbucks executive Schultz to step aside, mulling 'range of options'
resumo: Starbucks Corp Executive Chairman Howard Schultz is stepping away from the coffee chain he built into a global powerhouse, putting management firmly into the hands of Chief Executive Kevin Johnson, who had an office connected to the co-founder's and regularly turned to Schultz in times of crisis.

titulo: Exclusive: ConocoPhillips prepares to sell stake in Canada's Cenovus - sources
resumo: ConocoPhillips  is preparing to offload its stake in Cenovus Energy Inc , which it acquired as part of an asset sale to the Canadian oil and gas producer last year, people familiar with the matter told Reuters.

titulo: Apple issues new App Store rules aimed at streaming PC-based games
resumo: Apple Inc  on Monday issued new review guidelines for its lucrative App Store platform, after it moved to block plans by Steam, the biggest distributor of PC-based video games, to extend its reach into iPhones and iPads.

titulo: Apple focuses on speed, parental controls in software upgrades
resumo: Apple Inc  on Monday unveiled software upgrades that would let older iPhones run faster, help parents limit their children's screen time and make its Siri voice assistant work more like a rival feature from Amazon.com .

titulo: SocGen to pay $1.3 billion to settle Libya, Libor probes
resumo: French bank Societe Generale  will pay $1.3 billion to resolve criminal and civil charges in the United States and France for bribing Gaddafi-era Libyan officials and manipulating the Libor interest rate benchmark, U.S. authorities said on Monday.

titulo: Ex-Goldman Sachs programer seeks to overturn code theft conviction
resumo: Former Goldman Sachs Group Inc programer Sergey Aleynikov on Monday filed a new challenge to his criminal conviction of stealing computer code from the investment bank when he left for another job.

titulo: Total virtual currency sales jump in 2018 but monthly trend slows: report
resumo: Virtual currency sales ballooned to $9.1 billion so far this year, exceeding the $6.6 billion total for all of 2017, a financial technology data provider reported on Monday, but it said the monthly trend actually showed a slowdown if the two biggest offerings are excluded.

titulo: Dollar falls, U.S. yields rise, political fears ebb in Europe
resumo: The dollar fell to a two-week low and Treasury yields climbed on Monday as political tensions in Europe eased, while Wall Street stocks gained with technology shares as investors bet on continued strong economic growth.

titulo: Tech rally helps Wall Street push higher, Nasdaq has record close
resumo: Wall Street's three major indexes rose on Monday, led by a rally in tech stocks, pushing the Nasdaq to a record closing high as investors bet on a continuation of strong economic growth, while falling oil prices weighed on the energy sector.

titulo: Most Americans oppose businesses refusing to serve gay people: Reuters/Ipsos poll
resumo: Nearly three quarters of U.S. adults believe that businesses should not have the right on religious grounds to deny services to customers based on their sexual orientation, a Reuters/Ipsos opinion poll released on Monday showed.

titulo: Twitter Inc to join the S&P 500, replacing Monsanto
resumo: Twitter Inc. will replace Monsanto in the S&P 500, S&P Dow Jones Indices said on Monday.

titulo: Canada faces significant risks from U.S. tax, trade policy: IMF
resumo: The International Monetary Fund (IMF) said on Monday that while Canada's economy has performed well, it faces significant risks including trade tensions and a potential hit to competitiveness from lower U.S. corporate taxes.

titulo: Former State Street executive faces U.S. fraud trial
resumo: A former executive at State Street Corp  went on trial on Monday on U.S. charges that he participated in a scheme to defraud the bank's clients by charging them secret commissions on billions of dollars in trades.

titulo: Legg Mason to pay $64 million to resolve U.S. allegations of Libya bribery scheme

titulo: Ryanair's UK pilot union threatens strike action over promotion system

titulo: 'They blocked the truck!' Cargo bandits disrupt e-commerce in Brazil
resumo: In a bunker-like room in a gritty suburb of Sao Paulo, retired police colonel Antonio Marin and a dozen employees track the location of hundreds of cargo trucks on a bank of monitors.

titulo: AirAsia shares tumble as India probe threatens IPO plans

titulo: China slams surprise U.S. trade announcement, says ready to fight

resumo: (Refiling item from May 25 to change name to 'Russell Mark' in 6th paragraph)

titulo: Exclusive: U.S. sorghum armada U-turns at sea after China tariffs

titulo: Nissan to cut hundreds of jobs at UK plant as diesel demand slides: source
resumo: Nissan  will cut hundreds of jobs at its Sunderland plant, Britain's biggest automotive factory, as it tackles declining demand for diesel models across Europe, a source told Reuters on Friday.

titulo: Takeda weighs raising Shire bid to more than 47 stg/share: sources
resumo: Japan's Takeda Pharmaceutical Co Ltd is considering raising its cash-and-stock bid for London-listed drug maker Shire Plc to approximately 47 pounds ($66) per share or more, people familiar with the matter said on Friday.

titulo: End of Sorrell's reign heralds change for big ad empires
resumo: Martin Sorrell's departure from the world's biggest advertising company heralds change for his sprawling WPP empire and may accelerate a shake up of the big ad groups that followed his lead.

titulo: WPP says no follow-up requests from UK watchdog over CEO investigation
resumo: WPP has had no further requests for information from Britain's financial watchdog after answering initial questions about the firm's investigation into Martin Sorrell, its recently departed chief executive, a company spokesman said.

titulo: Porsche's head of powertrain development arrested, source says

titulo: Air France launches new talks to end strike, with chief's job at stake
resumo: Air France  launched on Friday a new consultation period with staff members to try to end a strike dispute over pay that has so far cost it 220 million euros ($271 million), with the company's head saying his job was on the line.

titulo: GM Korea's board delays decision on bankruptcy filing to Monday: sources
resumo: The board of General Motors'  South Korean unit delayed its decision on filing for court-led rehabilitation proceedings to Monday, said two sources familiar with the situation.

titulo: Exclusive: Lockheed Martin to propose stealthy hybrid of F-22 and F-35 for Japan - sources
resumo: U.S. defense contractor Lockheed Martin Corp plans to offer Japan a stealth fighter design based on its export-banned F-22 Raptor and advanced F-35 Lightning II aircraft, two sources said.

titulo: Facebook's damage limitation drive hits trouble in Germany
resumo: Facebook's attempt to limit fallout from a massive data breach hit trouble in Germany on Friday as a privacy watchdog opened a case against the social network and politicians accused its bosses of evasion.

titulo: U.S. companies upbeat about business in Germany: survey
resumo: Some 82 percent of U.S. companies operating in Germany expect revenues to increase this year, a survey showed on Friday, despite a brewing trade conflict over the U.S. decision to impose tariffs steel and aluminum imports.

titulo: South Korea's antitrust chief says expects change in Samsung governance 'in near future'

titulo: In Vietnam, a mega casino rises with the help of a Macau junket company

titulo: Barclays chief Staley survives whistleblowing inquiry with fines

titulo: China trade body urges tighter corporate compliance after ZTE woes: document

titulo: Asian oil demand to hit record, but industry can't take eyes off Middle East
resumo: Asian oil demand will hit a record in April just as global crude values are lifted to levels not seen in three years by Middle East supply risks and top exporter Saudi Arabia withholding output and noisily pushing for prices at $80 to $100 per barrel.

titulo: Daimler recalls 24,763 Mercedes-Benz cars in Russia - Russian watchdog
resumo: Russia's standards agency said on Friday it had been informed about the voluntary recall by German carmaker Daimler  of 24,763 Mercedes-Benz cars sold from 2014 to 2017.

titulo: U.S. Treasury weighs emergency powers to curb Chinese investments: official
resumo: The U.S. Treasury is considering ways to restrict sensitive Chinese investments in the United States by invoking an emergency powers law and bringing forward some security review reforms for corporate acquisitions, a senior Treasury official said on Thursday.

titulo: Russia gives green light to Bayer's takeover of Monsanto: Ifax
resumo: Russia's anti-monopoly regulator FAS has approved Bayer's  takeover of Monsanto , Russian news agency Interfax reported on Friday, citing the regulator.

titulo: China Huarong confirms chairman departs after probe, shares tumble

titulo: JPMorgan, National Bank of Canada, others test debt issuance on blockchain
resumo: JPMorgan Chase & Co  has tested a new blockchain platform for issuing financial instruments with the National Bank of Canada and other large firms, they said on Friday, seeking to streamline origination, settlement, interest rate payments and other processes.

titulo: Trade tensions with U.S. testing resolve of Chinese consumers
resumo: Guo Qingshan delights in riding his 400,000 yuan ($63,839) Harley-Davidson  motorbike around Beijing's suburbs.

titulo: As GM mulls options for unit, how does bankruptcy work in South Korea?
resumo: General Motors  has said its loss-making South Korean operation would file for bankruptcy if its union failed to agree to cut labour costs by Friday, heaping pressure on workers and the South Korean government to approve a rescue plan.

titulo: BRF shareholders with 1/3 stake agree on next board of directors
resumo: Shareholders with a combined 32.8 percent stake in Brazilian food processor BRF SA  have agreed to jointly vote for 10 new members of the board of directors, led by Pedro Parente as chairman, BRF said on Thursday in a filing.

resumo: Consumer activists and technology experts are advising consumers to hold off on buying Android handsets from China's ZTE until the company clarifies whether U.S. sanctions against the company prevent it from providing operating system updates for its devices.

titulo: Exclusive: China nears first Americas refining capacity as CNPC, Petrobras talks mature - sources
resumo: Brazil's state-run oil company is nearing a deal in which China National Petroleum Corp Ltd (CNPC) would invest in an oil refinery in exchange for crude oil, two people with knowledge of the talks told Reuters, potentially giving China its first refining capacity in the Americas.

titulo: Shire rejects $63 billion Takeda bid as Allergan drops pursuit
resumo: Rare disease drug maker Shire Plc  said on Thursday it had rejected a $63 billion cash-and-stock acquisition offer by Japan's Takeda Pharmaceutical Co Ltd , while Allergan Plc  reversed course on pursuing a rival bid.

resumo: Canadian, Mexican and U.S. ministers seeking to revamp the North American Free Trade Agreement (NAFTA) have made good progress on the key question of autos, Canadian Foreign Minister Chrystia Freeland said Thursday as pressure for a deal intensified.

titulo: Mattel names former Maker Studios executive as CEO after Georgiadis quits
resumo: Mattel Inc  said on Thursday that board member and digital media executive Ynon Kreiz will replace Chief Executive Margaret Georgiadis, who is stepping down after only 14 months at the helm of the maker of Hot Wheels and Barbie dolls.

titulo: Wells Fargo nears $1 billion settlement for loan abuses: source

titulo: Amazon says it has more than 100 million Prime members

titulo: Qualcomm cutting 1,500 jobs at its California offices
resumo: Chipmaker Qualcomm Inc  is cutting 1,500 jobs across multiple divisions at its offices in California, as part of its promise to investors to cut annual costs by $1 billion.

titulo: Wall Street tumbles on tobacco and tech, commodities soften after wild ride
resumo: Tobacco and tech stocks dragged down Wall Street on Thursday, while oil prices softened and other commodities walked back huge gains after a wild ride.

titulo: New York's 'Fearless Girl' to stare down the Stock Exchange
resumo: The bronze statue of a little girl that became a tourism phenomenon by staring down Wall Street's massive "Charging Bull" sculpture is to be moved to a nearby spot where its stern gaze will be on the male-dominated New York Stock Exchange, city officials said on Thursday.

titulo: Trudeau says recognizes NAFTA timeline, will stick up for Canada
resumo: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau said on Thursday he recognized the timelines on seeking a NAFTA trade agreement and was engaging as much as possible to meet an opportunity to make "significant progress".

titulo: Trump launches effort to boost U.S. weapons sales abroad

titulo: Exclusive: Facebook to put 1.5 billion users out of reach of new EU privacy law

resumo: Wage talks between General Motors'  South Korean unit and its labor union ended without an agreement on Thursday, a day before the deadline the company had set for filing for bankruptcy.

titulo: Nissan premium brand Infiniti aims to triple China sales: president
resumo: (This story corrects headline to show title is "president" (not CEO).)

resumo: (This April 17 story corrects first paragraph to show Trillium is an asset manager not an activist hedge fund)

Just two months ago, bankers and investors were tentatively talking of a figure closer to $100 billion.

titulo: Morgan Stanley executives tamp down enthusiasm on record profit
resumo: Morgan Stanley  reported record first-quarter profit on Wednesday thanks to a surge in trading activity, much like other Wall Street banks, but executives warned results through the rest of the year may not be quite as strong.

resumo: Amazon.com Inc  announced a new feature within its Amazon shopping app to cater to international customers shopping for U.S. goods, targeting Brazil, China, Germany and Spanish-speaking markets.

resumo: Abbott Laboratories'  just beat expectations for first quarter profits and revenue on Wednesday but stuck to a full-year profit forecast some investors had expected would be raised, sending the healthcare company's shares lower.

titulo: German prosecutors investigate Porsche board member over emissions
resumo: German prosecutors are investigating current and former employees of Volkswagen-owned sports car maker Porsche, including a management board member, as part of their inquiries into emissions manipulations, they said on Wednesday.

titulo: Porsche AG CEO not targeted in German emissions probe: sources
resumo: Porsche AG's  Chief Executive Oliver Blume is not the board member targeted by Stuttgart prosecutors probing an emissions cheating scandal, two sources familiar with the talks said on Wednesday.

titulo: Whiskey sour? China importers fret over U.S. trade battle
resumo: When spirits dealer Daniel Taytslin brought his first pallet of American Ragtime Rye whiskey into Shanghai in early April, the 672 bottles faced a 5 percent import tariff at China's customs.

titulo: Shire faces criticism on CEO pay policy as crunch week looms
resumo: Shire , the $47 billion rare disease drugmaker being stalked by Japan's Takeda Pharmaceutical , faces fresh criticism over its executive pay policy from a leading investor advisory group as a crunch week approaches.

resumo: Lockheed Martin Corp's  Sikorsky has partnererd with more German firms in its bid to win a 4 billion euro ($5 billion) heavy-lift helicopter contract from Berlin, and will showcase the industrial team at next week's Berlin air show.

titulo: Carl Icahn buys stake in software provider VMware: CNBC
resumo: Billionaire investor Carl Icahn has acquired a stake in cloud computing platform VMware Inc , CNBC reported on Tuesday.

titulo: China's ZTE may lose Android license as U.S. market woes build
resumo: Chinese smartphone maker ZTE Corp's U.S. woes deepened on Tuesday, as regulators proposed new rules that could cut into its sales, while a supply ban means it may not be able to use Android software in its devices, according to a source.

titulo: Arming the world: Inside Trump's 'Buy American' drive to expand weapons exports

titulo: Toys 'R' Us turns down bid for stores from toy maker: source

titulo: Toshiba says unable to meet end-March deadline for chip unit sale

titulo: Trade conflict fears to keep markets on edge for weeks

titulo: Take Five: World markets themes for the week ahead

resumo: (This version of the March 27 story corrects to "Minneapolis" from "Helena, Montana" in paragraph 3)

titulo: Tesla denies Musk in talks with Israeli AI vision firm Cortica

titulo: Index provider MSCI says it is reviewing Facebook data privacy issue
